jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Integral Senior Living (ISL) proudly manages care and lifestyle-focused Independent Living, Assisted Living and Memory Care communities across California and surrounding areas serving thousands of residents and families throughout the region. Guided by our foundational “Culture Keepers”, we cultivate purpose-driven environments where residents thrive and Team Members feel valued, empowered, and supported.

As part of one of the largest senior living organizations in the nation, our communities have earned Great Place to Work® certifications from 2022–2026. At ISL, purpose fuels every role, culture inspires every team, and continuous growth shapes every career. We continually aspire and invest in learning and development opportunities that support our team members’ professional growth, ensuring every team member has the tools to build a meaningful and rewarding career.

Integral Senior Living is looking for an Executive Director to join our community Discovery Commons San Ramon.

About The Opportunity

Does working for the leader in the Senior Housing industry sound like the professional challenge you are seeking? Integral Senior Living offers significant opportunities for growth and career advancement. Employees are encouraged to take responsibility for their own careers. You'll be working with incredible individuals with boundless creativity.

Responsibilities

Responsible for the operation and management of the community in accordance with the standards and guidelines of the Owners/Board of Directors, Integral Management Group, and federal/state/local laws and regulations.
Communicates and models a clear, customer focused vision, based upon a resident centered model of care.
Demonstrates the ability to identify and build relationships within the local area that drives businesses into the community, as well as effectively price the product, thereby maximizing top-line revenue growth and achieves appropriate market position.
Ensures the community has an effective external business development strategy in place, with clear accountabilities assigned, is able to articulate results and adjust plan accordingly to maximize referral leads and move-ins.
Develops a thorough working knowledge of state regulations, policies and procedures dictated for residents; ensures compliance.
Ensures all resident administrative files are well maintained, current and in compliance with state regulations.
Meets the financial targets with the goal to maximize capital partners’ return.
Functional knowledge of all operating programs including memory care, clinical, dining and social programs.
Prepares, adheres to the community budget.
Ensures budgeted revenue is achieved or exceeded by maximizing occupancy and room rate.
Reviews monthly financial statements, implements plans of action for deficiencies.
Manages key, non-labor operating costs in line with budgeted levels.
Processes and submits monthly expenses and budget data timely per policies and internal business controls.
Meets NOI and occupancy expectations.
Oversees all departments, maintaining full responsibility for efficient operations and compliance with the financial goals established in the approved Operating Budget.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree, preferably in Business Administration, Hospitality, Hospital Administration, or Health related field.
Minimum of three years of managerial experience preferably in the senior living, health care or hospitality industries.
Previous management experience including hiring, coaching, performance management, daily operations supervision and discipline.
Previous sales experience preferred.
Demonstration of success in managing operating expenses.
Administrator License/certification
Provides leadership by exhibiting confidence in self and others; inspires and motivates others to perform well.

Benefits

In addition to a rewarding career and competitive salary, Integral offers a comprehensive benefit package.

Eligible team members are offered a comprehensive benefit package including medical, dental, vision, life and disability insurances, paid time off and paid holidays. Team members are eligible to participate in our outstanding 401(k) plan with company match our Employee Assistance Program and accident insurance policies.

No agencies, please. We do not accept any unsolicited resumes from agencies under any circumstances. We receive inquiries from agencies daily. Agencies should not direct any inquiries or emails to hiring managers. Thank you.

jobs.title: Program Director
jobs.description:
Superlanet is seeking an experienced Program Director, Hospital IT Managed Services to lead the delivery of managed IT services across our healthcare client portfolio. This leadership role oversees multiple client engagements, ensures operational excellence and exceptional service delivery, and serves as a trusted advisor to health system executives.

The ideal candidate has a strong background in healthcare IT operations, managed services, and leading cross-functional technical teams within hospital environments. Working closely with Superlanet leadership, this individual will drive client success, optimize service delivery, support team development, and help grow our managed services practice.

Responsibilities

· Lead the delivery of hospital IT managed services across Superlanet's healthcare client portfolio, ensuring operational excellence and exceptional client satisfaction.
· Ensure the quality, efficiency, and consistency of managed IT services delivered across all client engagements.
· Build trusted relationships with CIOs, IT executives, and operational leaders, serving as a strategic advisor for managed services delivery.
· Ensure compliance with service level agreements, monitor key performance indicators, and implement corrective actions when performance expectations are not met.
· Manage the onboarding of new managed services clients in partnership with recruiting, operations, account management, and technical leadership teams.
· Continuously evaluate, develop, and improve service management processes to increase operational efficiency and service quality.
· Partner with Client Success Managers, Account Managers, and Technical Advisors to ensure a high level of client satisfaction and long-term partnership success.
· Provide executive oversight for major healthcare IT initiatives, including Epic implementations, optimization projects, enterprise technology transformations, and operational readiness activities.
· Manage critical client communications related to major incidents, service disruptions, escalations, outages, and executive-level concerns.
· Produce, analyze, and present operational performance reports, identifying trends and recommending service improvements.
· Manage program financials, staffing plans, resource allocation, forecasting, and overall account health.
· Drive issue resolution, risk management, and continuous service improvement across all managed services engagements.
· Identify opportunities to expand existing client relationships through additional managed services, strategic consulting, and technology initiatives.

Requirements

Qualifications

· Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Healthcare Administration, Business, or a related field (Master's preferred).
· 8+ years of healthcare IT leadership experience.
· 5+ years leading managed services, healthcare IT operations, or enterprise support organizations.
· Experience supporting hospitals, health systems, or academic medical centers.
· Strong knowledge of healthcare technologies, including Epic or other enterprise EHR platforms, infrastructure, cloud services, networking, cybersecurity, endpoint management, and IT operations.
· Proven experience managing multiple client engagements, cross-functional teams, budgets, and executive-level relationships.
· Excellent communication, leadership, organizational, and client relationship management skills.
· PMP, ITIL, or similar leadership certifications are preferred.

Preferred Experience

· Experience supporting multiple health systems simultaneously.
· Epic implementation, optimization, or managed services leadership.
· Strong understanding of ITIL, operational governance, service delivery, and continuous improvement methodologies.

Benefits
Location:
Remote with travel
Salary Range:
$180,000–$225,000

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Scion Executive Search has been engaged to conduct an immediate search for an Executive Director
for our client, a national nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing research, education, and public awareness surrounding skin health and dermatologic disease. Through strategic partnerships with leading researchers, physicians, academic institutions, donors, and volunteers, they accelerate innovative research while improving the lives of individuals affected by skin disease.

We are seeking a collaborative, mission-driven professional to lead the organization through its next chapter of growth, ensuring operational excellence, financial sustainability, and continued impact across research, fundraising, and organizational leadership. This full-time, hybrid role (3 days/week in office) is based in New York, New York.

Position Overview

Reporting to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director serves as the organization's chief executive, providing strategic, operational, and financial leadership while championing the mission of the organization. This leader will oversee daily operations, cultivate donor and institutional relationships, partner closely with the Board, and lead fundraising initiatives that support the organization's long-term success.

The Executive Director will manage a lean, high-performing team, including a Project Manager, while serving as the organization's primary ambassador to donors, researchers, clinicians, corporate partners, and other external stakeholders.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

Executive Leadership & Strategy

Provide visionary leadership that advances the organization's mission, strategic priorities, and long-term sustainability.
Partner closely with the Board of Directors to establish organizational goals, priorities, and annual operating plans.
Translate strategic objectives into measurable initiatives and organizational outcomes.
Foster a collaborative, mission-focused culture centered on accountability, transparency, and continuous improvement.

Fundraising & External Relations

Lead the organization's fundraising strategy, including major gifts, corporate partnerships, foundations, and special events.
Cultivate, steward, and expand relationships with donors, philanthropic partners, and key supporters.
Serve as the public face of the organization, representing the organization with researchers, medical institutions, corporate sponsors, and community stakeholders.
Identify opportunities to increase organizational visibility and philanthropic engagement.

Operations & Financial Leadership

Oversee the organization's daily operations while ensuring efficient, effective organizational performance.
Develop and manage the annual operating budget in partnership with the Board and external financial partners.
Ensure strong financial stewardship, reporting, compliance, and sound business practices.
Establish operational processes that support organizational growth and scalability.

Team Leadership

Lead, mentor, and develop a small, dedicated staff while fostering a collaborative and high-performing work environment.
Directly supervise the Project Manager and support professional growth across the organization.
Recruit, retain, and develop talent that strengthens organizational capacity.

Board Partnership & Governance

Serve as the primary liaison to the Board of Directors.
Prepare Board materials, reports, financial updates, and strategic recommendations.
Support Board committees and governance initiatives while ensuring effective communication and engagement.

QUALIFICATIONS:

Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred.
Progressive executive leadership experience within a nonprofit organization, foundation, healthcare, medical research, or related mission-driven environment.
Demonstrated success leading fundraising initiatives and cultivating major donor relationships.
Strong financial and operational management experience, including budgeting, planning, and organizational oversight.
Experience partnering with an engaged Board of Directors and navigating nonprofit governance.
Outstanding relationship-building, communication, and executive presence.
Strategic thinker with the ability to balance long-term vision and day-to-day execution.
Passion for advancing medical research, healthcare, or scientific innovation is strongly preferred.

The successful candidate will be:
A mission-driven executive who inspires confidence among donors, Board members, staff, and external partners.
A relationship builder who excels at cultivating meaningful partnerships and securing philanthropic support.
A strategic and operational leader capable of strengthening organizational infrastructure while driving future growth.
An effective communicator with exceptional executive presence and emotional intelligence.
A collaborative leader who empowers staff while maintaining accountability and high performance.

COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS:

In addition to offering a competitive salary in the range of $180,000 to $200,000 (commensurate with experience), this opportunity provides a comprehensive benefit package, to be disclosed.

HOW TO APPLY:

For immediate consideration please provide your resume and cover letter, sharing more about your experience and interest in this role via https://scionstaffing.com/job/15131/.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Global Fin Ops and Compliance - NYC
jobs.description:
Comcast Advertising is driving the TV advertising industry forward, from delivering ads to linear and digital audiences to pioneering the tech that makes it possible. We help brands connect with their audiences on every screen using advanced data, technology, and premium video content. Our media sales division helps local, regional, and national brands reach potential customers through multiscreen TV advertising. Our ad tech division FreeWheel provides comprehensive adtech that makes it easier to buy and sell premium video advertising across all screens, data types, and sales channels.

Job Summary

FreeWheel, a Comcast company, is seeking a seasoned and strategic finance leader to serve as Executive Director, Global Financial Operations & Compliance. Reporting directly to the CFO, this role sits at the center of our global growth strategy—driving operational excellence, financial integrity, and scalable governance across our global footprint. The Executive Director, FreeWheel Global Financial Operations & Compliance is responsible for setting the strategy, governance and execution of FreeWheel’s Global Financial Operations & Compliance function. Creates financial policies, procedures, controls and reporting systems. Executes accurate and timely billing for all FreeWheel services. Develops strategy to control potential credit losses and maximize collections. Ensures compliance with tax, regulatory, statutory and company requirements for several international subsidiaries. Demonstrates leadership versatility. Schedule: Onsite 4 days per week from the New York office. This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship. Applicants must be authorized to work for Comcast in the United States without a current or potential future need for sponsorship.

Job Description

Our Team:

The FreeWheel Finance team has strong business partnerships and influence across the organization. We drive financially informed decision-making in support of leadership’s strategic priorities, while continuously improving processes to deliver an efficient transactional infrastructure for both internal and external customers.

The FreeWheel Finance leadership team has a proven track record of developing talent. We build nimble, business-fluent teams that create meaningful impact in achieving the company’s growth objectives.

Core Responsibilities

Ensures accurate and timely client invoicing and provides critical data for revenue and cost of services reporting.
Manages the development of business strategy to attempt to control potential credit losses and works with leadership to execute the business strategy.
Ensures FreeWheel is adequately protected against credit loss as it expands relationships with Agency Hold Co and Independent Agency clients.
Analyzes credit exposure (aging) reports and provides forecasts related to bad debt and write-offs.
Ensures compliance to all Federal, State and Country level invoicing, tax and reporting requirements.
Leads FreeWheel response to international tax and statutory audits.
Manages international cash movement among subsidiaries in accordance with Comcast policies and objectives.
Manages transfer pricing agreements among legal entities.
Owns global financial controls and governance, ensuring compliance with SOX, Comcast internal control standards, and enterprise risk management requirements.
Oversees usage of outside collection agencies.
Manages and motivates a global team, overseeing career growth and maximizing performance.
Demonstrates sound business judgment, financial acumen and leadership.

Employees At All Levels Are Expected To

Understand our Operating Principles; make them the guidelines for how you do your job.
Own the customer experience - think and act in ways that put our customers first, give them seamless digital options at every touchpoint, and make them promoters of our products and services.
Know your stuff - be enthusiastic learners, users and advocates of our game-changing technology, products and services, especially our digital tools and experiences.
Win as a team - make big things happen by working together and being open to new ideas.
Be an active part of the Net Promoter System - a way of working that brings more employee and customer feedback into the company - by joining huddles, making call backs and helping us elevate opportunities to do better for our customers.
Drive results and growth.
Support a culture of inclusion in how you work and lead.
Do what's right for each other, our customers, investors and our communities.

Disclaimer:

Comcast is an equal opportunity workplace. We will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other basis protected by applicable law.

Financial Operations; Transfer Pricing; Operational Excellence; Process Improvements

jobs.title: Chief of Staff to the CEO
jobs.description:
About Fubo:

FuboTV Inc. is a consumer-first live TV streaming company that is defining the future of TV.

Ranked among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies (2026) and the Financial Times’ The Americas’ Fastest-Growing Companies (2026, 2025), FuboTV owns Hulu + Live TV (entertainment), Fubo (sports) and Molotov (entertainment and sports), which stream in markets around the globe.

Our Mission:

Our mission is to deliver premium sports, news and entertainment programming through a best-in-class user experience that offers greater choice, flexibility and value.

About the Role:

This role is a New York City based HYBRID position. Candidates must be located in NYC, and willing to come into the office on a hybrid basis, three times a week (Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday).*

Fubo is currently seeking a Chief of Staff to the CEO to serve as a strategic partner in a new position. This is a high impact role that will help transform vision into execution across the company. It’s best suited for someone that thrives at the intersection of strategy and execution, excels at solving complex business problems, creates alignment across teams, and helps ensure the company’s most important priorities become a reality.

This is not a traditional leadership role with a defined team. It is a role built on influence without authority, working across the entire organization, acting as an extension of the CEO. You will bring structure to ambiguity, connecting teams, identifying opportunities for growth and improvement, and ensuring the leadership team is operating at its highest level.

What you will do:

Act as a trusted partner to the CEO to define, refine, and execute the company’s strategic agenda
Develop agenda’s for CEO lead meetings (Senior leadership meetings, board meetings, off-sites, etc.); Work with the CEO to identify key themes and topics, giving input as needed from key stakeholders
Coordinate other CEO lead events, and assist in developing content and agendas
Help translate long-term vision into clear initiatives, measurable objectives, and execution plans
Identify emerging opportunities and risks through market research, competitor analysis, and financial analysis
Ensure all materials shared with the CEO are succinct, accurate, current, and verified
Work with the finance team to support long-range planning, annual planning, and resource allocation
Build business cases and financial models to evaluate strategic investments and growth opportunities
Monitor the company’s performance against strategic objectives and identify areas requiring intervention
Help develop action plans and mobilizing resources to drive resolution for critical business challenges
Help improve how the executive team operates through better planning, decision making, and follow through
Partner closely with various business leaders across the organization to accelerate initiatives, remove roadblocks, and ensure teams are aligned by removing friction between teams, clarifying priorities, resolving dependencies, and improving communication
Build trust across organizations without relying on formal authority
Lead confidential and high priority CEO initiatives including partnerships, organizational design, investor preparation, board materials, and business transformation efforts
Help create clarity in ambiguous situations
Partner with People Ops and Talent Acquisition teams to support leadership development, succession planning, and organizational design
Represent the CEO in select areas and initiatives as needed
Anticipate business needs, challenges, and opportunities, and proactively provide insights and recommendations

Who you are:

You combine strategic thinking with practical execution
You’re comfortable discussing market expansion, financial performance, organizational design, and execution details
10+ years of experience in strategy consulting, corporate strategy, project management, business operations, or related leadership roles; preferably within the digital media, technology, or subscription space
Demonstrated success leading complex, cross-functional initiatives from ideation to execution
Experience working with executive leadership in fast moving organizations
A strong financial background with the ability to interpret data and build robust business cases
Outstanding analytical and problem solving skills
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, including written narratives, presentations, briefing materials, and leadership updates
High emotional intelligence, sound judgement, and absolute discretion
Highly structured and organised, with the ability to create clarity in varying situations
The ability to move comfortably between executive strategy and hands-on execution
Extensive experience working cross functionally with all levels of global business units (finance, marketing, technology, product, design, business development, etc.)
A proven ability to navigate complex stakeholder environments

Perks & Benefits:

At Fubo, you will have the opportunity to personally influence what live media looks like after it goes through its next big transformation
Fubo provides a highly competitive compensation based on experience and market standards
Robust benefit package including Health/Dental/Vision coverage 401k, Life Insurance, and commuter benefits
Free Premium Fubo Account
Unlimited PTO days and regular company-wide activities
Fubo's main headquarters are located in Midtown Manhattan, with offices in Paris, and Bangalore
Fubo is an e-verified company

Fubo’s minimum base salary for this role if hired in New York City is $185,000 per year; maximum base salary for this role is $250,000 per year. Additionally, this role is eligible to participate in Fubo's equity plan, annual cash bonus plans, unlimited PTO, and a full range of medical benefits. The final offer amount will be at the company’s sole discretion and determined by multiple factors, including years and depth of experience and expertise, and other business considerations.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Consumer Data Strategy & Operations
jobs.description:
The Estée Lauder Companies Inc.
is one of the world’s leading manufacturers, marketers, and sellers of quality skin care, makeup, fragrance, and hair care products, and is a steward of luxury and prestige brands globally. The company’s products are sold in approximately 150 countries and territories under brand names including: Estée Lauder, Aramis, Clinique, Lab Series, Origins, M·A·C, La Mer, Bobbi Brown Cosmetics, Aveda, Jo Malone London, Bumble and bumble, Darphin Paris, TOM FORD, Smashbox, AERIN Beauty, Le Labo, Editions de Parfums Frédéric Malle, GLAMGLOW, KILIAN PARIS, Too Faced, Dr.Jart+, the DECIEM family of brands, including The Ordinary and NIOD, and BALMAIN Beauty.

Description

Executive Director, Consumer Data Strategy & Operations

Role Overview

The Executive Director, Consumer Data Strategy & Operations will lead the development of ELC's consumer data capabilities, audience foundations and governance frameworks as part of the new Growth Marketing & media organization. This role is responsible for establishing the enterprise capabilities required to enable audience-led marketing across ELC, ensuring consumer data can be consistently activated, and measured.

Working closely with IT and Privacy teams, the Executive Director will define the standards, frameworks, and capabilities required to unlock greater value from ELC's first-party data assets and enable future growth across paid and owned channels. The role will establish a common approach to consumer data management, audience governance and segmentation, creating the foundations required for more effective marketing execution.

Qualifications

Establish ELC's Consumer Data Strategy & Foundations
Define the enterprise vision and roadmap for consumer data capabilities aligned to ELC's broader marketing transformation.
Establish global consumer data standards, taxonomies, and foundational frameworks that can be adopted consistently across brands and affiliates.
Partner closely with Privacy, Legal, Security, and IT teams to ensure consumer data practices align with global regulatory requirements and internal governance standards.
Partner closely with other functions, regions and affiliates to define and prioritize global use cases to inform IT roadmaps.
Drive value realization from consumer data investments through strong adoption, governance, and cross-functional collaboration.

Define Global Audience Segmentation Frameworks

Lead development of ELC's global segmentation framework, creating a common approach that can be adopted across brands, categories, and markets.
Define methodologies for identifying high-value consumer groups and monitor performance insights over time.
Partner to ensure segmentation and audience capabilities are embedded within media planning, audience strategy, and measurement frameworks.

Build Global Consumer Data Operating Model

Define the future-state operating model for consumer data across global, regional, and local teams.
Establish global governance frameworks for audience creation, management, activation, and measurement across global EBS and media agency partners.
Develop scalable ways of working that improve accessibility, usability, and adoption of consumer data capabilities across the enterprise.

Capability Building & Organizational Enablement

Build enterprise understanding of consumer data, audience management, segmentation, identity, and governance principles.
Create capability-building programs, playbooks, and communities of practice that accelerate adoption of audience-led marketing across ELC.
Establish a global community of consumer data practitioners to share best practices, learnings, and innovation opportunities.
Drive adoption of common consumer data standards and audience capabilities across brands and affiliates.
Build and mentor a high-performing team while fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, governance, and continuous improvement.

Pay Range

Anticipated Base Salary Range $158,650.00 to $272,700.00 (Depending on qualifications, skills, experience and/or budget), based on a 40 hour work week (range to be scaled accordingly). In addition, The Estée Lauder Companies offers a variety of benefits to eligible employees, including health insurance coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance), wellness and family support programs, life and disability insurance, retirement savings plans, education-related programs, paid holidays and vacation time. In addition, the Company maintains highly competitive incentive compensation programs (role eligibility may vary based on terms of the respective plan(s)).

You may be eligible to participate in the applicable Commission/Bonus Plan, under the plan guidelines in effect at the time of hire. Additional details regarding the commission plan will be provided as part of your onboarding.

Equal Opportunity Employer

We are an equal-opportunity employer. Minorities, women, veterans, and individuals with disabilities are encouraged to apply. Accommodations for job applicants with disabilities are available on request.

The Estée Lauder Companies Inc.
is one of the world’s leading manufacturers, marketers, and sellers of quality skin care, makeup, fragrance, and hair care products, and is a steward of luxury and prestige brands globally. The company’s products are sold in approximately 150 countries and territories under brand names including: Estée Lauder, Aramis, Clinique, Lab Series, Origins, M·A·C, La Mer, Bobbi Brown Cosmetics, Aveda, Jo Malone London, Bumble and bumble, Darphin Paris, TOM FORD, Smashbox, AERIN Beauty, Le Labo, Editions de Parfums Frédéric Malle, GLAMGLOW, KILIAN PARIS, Too Faced, Dr.Jart+, the DECIEM family of brands, including The Ordinary and NIOD, and BALMAIN Beauty.

Description

Role Overview

The Executive Director, Influencer Marketing partners with global brands and affiliates to operationalize and elevate influencer marketing across all ELC brands. This role is responsible for developing frameworks to identify and engage different tiers of influencers, enabling the selection and adoption of the right global technology platforms for discovery and performance measurement, and designing the operating model including agency partnerships and market collaboration that supports consistent, scalable execution.

The Executive Director ensures influencer marketing is embedded as a strategic enabler of our new global Growth Marketing practice, delivering brand equity and measurable business impact across the enterprise.

Qualifications

Key Responsibilities

Influencer Strategy & Frameworks

Define enterprise influencer marketing strategy, with a clear global vision to accelerate adoption and value realization across brands and affiliates.
Establish global influencer tiering models, playbooks and activation frameworks, to outline best practices for brands and affiliates to embed influencer into their overall brand ecosystem with clear roles and KPIs.
Define global investment guidance frameworks aligned to market maturity, channel roles, and category growth ambitions.
Build competitive intelligence and benchmarking frameworks including share of voice, creator mix, and performance benchmarks.
Collaborate closely with media and social commerce teams to embed influencer best practices and frameworks as part of an integrated communications strategy.

Technology, Measurement & Performance

Partner closely with IT and global affiliates to identify and scale global influencer technology platforms for discovery, management, activation and measurement.
Establish global affiliate technologies globally (eg. Shop my) and work closely with performance marketing team to embed insights into our total performance approach.
Define global KPI frameworks and reporting standards across brand and performance objectives, with clear monitoring to track global performance and adoption.
Define standardized global KPI taxonomy across brand, engagement, commerce and ROI metrics.

Operating Model & Governance

Enable the new OneELC global operating model for influencer marketing, defining roles and responsibilities across global brands, regions, and local teams.
Establish global roster of influencer agencies and work with global affiliates to embed as part of their local ways of working.
Partner with legal to establish global agency contracting framework and incentive model to unlock full potential from agency partners.
Establish clear governance, guidelines, and best practices covering brand safety, compliance, disclosures, contracts, and usage rights in close partnership with global legal teams.

Market Enablement & Capability Building

Develop global influencer community across ELC, cascading global best practices, learnings and feedback on global frameworks.

Build and mentor a high‑performing global team and extended community of influencer practitioners.

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Director of Operations

Brooklyn, New York | Hybrid

Compensation:
$175,000–$200,000, commensurate with experience, plus a performance-based bonus opportunity of up to 20% of base salary tied to agreed-upon individual and Firm performance objectives.

Help Build New York's Premier Boutique Business Litigation Firm

At Alisme Law, we believe exceptional legal service begins with exceptional operations.
We represent businesses, business owners, partners, and shareholders in complex business disputes. Our clients trust us because we make sense of complexity through disciplined systems, thoughtful strategy, and consistent execution.
As our Firm continues to grow, we are intentionally building an organization that is every bit as exceptional operationally as it is legally.
We are seeking an experienced Director of Operations to serve as the operational leader of our Firm. This is an opportunity for someone who enjoys building teams, developing leaders, improving systems, and creating an organization where people consistently perform at a high level.
If you are energized by building organizations, not simply managing them, we would love to meet you.

Where We Are Going

Alisme Law has built a strong reputation for helping clients navigate complex business disputes. Our next chapter is focused on building a scalable organization capable of sustained growth while maintaining the personalized service, professionalism, and trust our clients expect.
Over the next several years, we will continue to strengthen our leadership team, refine our operating systems, and invest in our people so the Firm can grow intentionally without sacrificing its culture or client experience.
We are entering an exciting stage of growth. Our Firm has built a strong foundation and is now investing in the leadership, systems, and infrastructure necessary to support our next phase. The Director of Operations will inherit responsibility for leading our administrative team while helping build the management structure, operational processes, and organizational capacity needed for sustained growth.
We are looking for an operational leader who is excited to help build that future.

About the Role

Reporting directly to the Owner, you will lead the Firm's day-to-day business operations by developing people, strengthening accountability, improving systems, and ensuring operational excellence across the organization.
This is not an office manager position. It is a senior leadership role for someone who enjoys coaching people, improving organizations, solving operational challenges, and building high-performing teams.
While the Owner establishes the Firm's strategic direction, the Director of Operations is responsible for leading the execution of that vision through people, systems, and operational discipline.

Your First-Year Priorities

During your first year, your primary focus will be to build the operational foundation for the Firm's next stage of growth.

Build a High-Performing Team

Recruit, onboard, develop, and coach exceptional people. Create a culture where every team member understands expectations, receives regular feedback, and is held accountable to measurable standards.

Build a Scalable Operating Model

Strengthen the Firm's operating systems by documenting and improving core processes, implementing meaningful KPIs, establishing consistent meeting rhythms, and ensuring operational excellence across the organization.

Deliver an Exceptional Client Experience

Improve the operational systems that support the client journey, from intake through matter completion, by creating consistency, responsiveness, and professionalism at every stage.

What You Will Own

To accomplish these priorities, you will lead the Firm's business operations, including:
People Operations
Business Operations
Client Experience
Operational Finance
Technology and Vendor Management

Your success will be measured not by how much work you personally complete, but by how effectively the organization performs under your leadership.

Decision-Making Authority

We believe leadership requires both responsibility and authority.
Within established budgets, policies, and strategic objectives, you will have the authority to:

Lead, coach, evaluate, and hold administrative team members accountable while ensuring firm-wide compliance with the Firm's operational systems, policies, and performance standards.
Recommend hiring, promotions, compensation adjustments, and terminations.
Manage recruiting, onboarding, and performance management.
Improve and implement operational systems, workflows, and standard operating procedures.
Select and manage vendors and recommend technology solutions.
Establish and monitor operational KPIs and management reporting.
Resolve day-to-day operational issues independently.
Lead strategic operational initiatives from planning through implementation.

The Owner retains responsibility for legal strategy, client representation, annual budgeting, pricing, compensation strategy, and other major strategic decisions.

As you develop a deep understanding of the Firm, earn the Owner's trust, and build a strong operational foundation, the Owner intends to progressively delegate responsibility for the Firm's day-to-day operations. The long-term vision is for the Director of Operations to lead the Firm's business operations, allowing the Owner to focus primarily on legal strategy, client relationships, business development, and the Firm's long-term growth.

Operational Financial Leadership

You will oversee the Firm's operational financial processes by ensuring disciplined billing, timekeeping, collections, trust replenishment, operational reporting, and KPI management.
You will partner closely with the Owner and the Firm's outside accounting professionals on budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, and business planning. While you will play an important role in supporting the Firm's financial health, responsibility for financial strategy remains with the Owner.

Client Experience

The Director of Operations owns the systems and processes that support an exceptional client experience. This includes intake, onboarding, communication standards, service workflows, billing experience, and client feedback systems.
You will work in partnership with the Owner to continuously improve the client experience while recognizing that legal strategy and attorney-client representation remain the responsibility of the Firm's attorneys.

The Director is responsible for establishing, monitoring, and improving the Firm's operational standards across all personnel. Attorneys remain responsible for legal judgment and client representation, while the Director oversees compliance with the Firm's operational systems and procedures.

What Success Looks Like After 12 Months

A successful first year will include meaningful progress in the following areas:

Leadership & People

A structured recruiting, onboarding, and performance management process is fully implemented.
Every employee has clear expectations, measurable KPIs, and regular coaching.
Leadership capacity is strengthened by developing team members, establishing clear accountability, and ensuring operational issues are addressed at the appropriate level within the organization.

Operations

Core operational workflows are documented, standardized, and consistently followed.
A meaningful operational KPI dashboard is implemented and reviewed regularly.
Weekly leadership meetings and quarterly planning sessions operate with consistency, accountability, and follow-through.
Operational decisions are supported by accurate reporting and meaningful performance data.

Client Experience

Client communication standards are consistently met.
Intake, onboarding, billing, and matter completion processes become more predictable and efficient.
Client feedback is actively measured and used to improve service delivery.

Business Operations

Billing, collections, and operational reporting processes operate consistently and efficiently.
Operational technology supports productivity and continuous improvement.
The Firm's leadership team has greater visibility into performance and operational trends through consistent reporting and accountability.

Compensation & Benefits

We believe exceptional performance should be rewarded. Our compensation package includes:

Competitive base salary of $175,000–$200,000, commensurate with experience.
Performance-based bonus opportunity of up to 20% of base salary, tied to agreed-upon individual and Firm performance objectives.
Hybrid work environment. During onboarding, the Director is expected to work primarily from our Brooklyn office to build relationships, learn the Firm, and understand our operations. Thereafter, we anticipate a flexible hybrid schedule with regular in-office leadership presence, generally one to two days per week, depending on business needs and Firm priorities. Additional in-person time may be expected during key hiring, planning, training, and leadership initiatives.
Paid Time Off and paid holidays.
Professional development opportunities.
The opportunity to help shape the future of a growing law firm.

Our Hiring Process

We hire carefully because our people are our greatest competitive advantage.
To apply please email the following to info@alismelaw.com
:
Your résumé.
A brief email explaining why this opportunity interests you.
A video (no longer than three minutes) answering the following questions:
Why does this opportunity excite you?
Tell us about an operational transformation you personally led that had a measurable impact.

Please include the phrase "Systems Create Freedom" in the subject line of your application.
Because attention to detail, communication, and follow-through are essential to success in this role, applicants who submit complete application materials and follow the requested instructions will receive priority consideration.

Please do not contact the Firm by telephone or visit our office regarding this position.

in St. Augustine, Florida.

Founded in 1954, Westminster Communities of Florida is the largest not-for-profit provider of senior living in the state dedicated to a Mission and ministry of service to older adults. We proudly serve more than 7,000 residents throughout Florida, offering an active lifestyle paired with a full continuum of exceptional care — including independent living, assisted living, memory support, skilled nursing and rehabilitation, home care, pharmacy services, and affordable housing for low-income seniors. Join a team where you can make life great for residents and team members alike!

What You'll Do

Leadership & Operations

Implement and maintain policies established by the Board of Directors and Community Support Services offices
Uphold organizational standards and ensure fulfillment of all contractual obligations to residents, including entrance fee and continuing care agreements
Ensure compliance with all applicable laws and regulations, including AHCA licensure requirements, state Life Plan Community/CCRC statutes, and CMS quality standards where applicable
Maintain survey readiness and support continuous quality improvement across all levels of care

Financial & Business Operations

Monitor budget performance, occupancy trends, and revenue cycle health; submit monthly variance reports and approve major purchases and invoices
Apply Life Plan/CCRC business acumen — understanding entrance fee models, reserve requirements, and actuarial considerations unique to Life Plan Community operations

Experience

Skills & Attributes

Strong ability to motivate and communicate effectively with residents, families, and staff across multiple levels of care

Self-motivated with a proven ability to set and achieve goals
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present confidently to leadership, boards, and community groups
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; able to interpret complex data and technical information
Resilient and adaptable under pressure, with the ability to navigate the mental and emotional demands of a dynamic senior living leadership role
Genuine passion for the aging services mission and improving the lived experience of older adults

Why Join Us

This is an opportunity to lead with purpose — shaping a community where residents feel valued, supported, and at home, and where staff are equipped to do their best work. You'll be backed by a strong Community Support Services infrastructure and a shared commitment to excellence across the full continuum of senior living.

jobs.description:
Organization Summary

Centro de Trabajadores Unidos en Lucha (CTUL) is a worker-led organization where workers organize, educate, and empower each other to fight for a voice in their workplaces and in their communities. We partner with other organizations and leaders to build a movement to win racial, gender, and economic justice. We identify the root causes of injustice and work to shift the balance of power between those who have it and those who don’t to improve the lives of our communities for present and future generations. Visit www.ctul.net to learn more.

Position Description

CTUL is seeking an experienced Deputy Director to oversee the organization’s organizational development, governance, personnel management and strategic planning. The Deputy Director will be a leader and a change agent in driving best practices in support of the organization’s mission. This role will be an advocate for staff experience, organizational agility and creating a learning culture. The Deputy Director will have deep experience with operational best practices and up-to-date online tools.

This is an outstanding opportunity for a manager with outstanding people skills and a proven track record of leadership and effective change management. They will join a successful mission-driven advocacy organization and help lead it to become even more efficient, impactful and nimble.

This position reports to the Executive Director.

What you’ll do

Strategy, Vision and Leadership:

Contribute to the development of CTUL’s strategic goals and objectives as part of the leadership team, and help guide the overall management of the organization.
In support of the CTUL’s  mission, promote a culture of high performance and continuous improvement that values learning, feedback and a commitment to quality throughout the organization.
Provide analytical support to CTUL management and staff including development of internal reporting capabilities.
Maintain a continuous line of communication with the Executive Director to support strategic decision-making on all critical issues.
Report to the board of directors on organizational performance metrics as needed by the Executive Director.

Team Development and Personnel Management

Supervise Directors and be accountable for directors functions
Supervise the HR Lead and be accountable for human resources functions including: training; staff development; compensation and benefits; employee relations; and performance evaluation, with a strong focus on diversity, equity and inclusion.
Oversee staff recruitment and hiring.
Managing leads and staff meeting planning and facilitation
Support the ED in the planning and facilitation of director meetings 
Oversee/Provide guidance on informal coaching conversations, corrective action, and progressive discipline
Support conflict resolution and workplace investigations as needed
Oversee best practices for terminations and approve terminations 
Create, update and execute organizational policies across the organization; and ensure that all systems that support CTUL staff are efficient, easily accessible and transparent.
In support of the CTUL’s mission, promote a culture of growth and continuous improvement that prioritizes learning, feedback and a commitment to innovation throughout the organization.
Oversee staff training and practice to build an inclusive, supportive organizational culture where everyone on the team feels valued and can thrive.
Represent CTUL management with the staff union, and cultivate a collegial, problem-solving relationship with the union based on partnership and trust.

Governance 

Support the Executive Director in maintaining robust organizational governance for CTUL’s member-led board of directors; provide staff leadership to Board committees; ensure compliance with nonprofit governance best practices; and support the Board as needed with training and capacity building.
Act as a key staff liaison to the Board of Directors, translating strategic vision into operational reality while ensuring all programs and fiscal activities remain in compliance with the highest legal and ethical standards.

Legal

Manage legal needs for CTUL, working with outside counsel as required.

Qualifications:

10 years experience in a similar position, or equivalent combination of education and experience
Proven track record of success leading organizational change and development within a growing organization
Superior personnel management ability, with strong mentoring and coaching experience on teams with diverse levels of expertise. Experience managing in organizations with unionized staff preferred.
Strong working knowledge of Collective Bargaining Agreements (CBA) and labor law principles
Excellent judgment and creative problem solving skills including the ability to lead a range of stakeholders in establishing a shared vision and executing it together
Entrepreneurial, flexible team player who can think experimentally and can take a learning approach to our work. 
Organized, detail-oriented and experienced in managing complex projects
Exceptional written, oral, interpersonal, and presentation skills and the ability to effectively interface with management, boards of directors, and staff
Passion for racial justice and for CTUL’s mission
Ability to periodically work on weekends and evenings, as needed.
Confidentiality, integrity, and a sense of humor.

Salary and Benefits

CTUL has a tiered salary range set by the Staff and Board based on the principles of economic justice and inclusion. This salaried position is currently compensated at  $87,550 per year. This full-time position will normally work 80  hours per pay period, including some work on evenings and weekends. Benefits include: 3 weeks paid vacation, sick days, 5 recuperation days, 19 paid holidays, 90% employer-paid medical and dental insurance for you and your immediate family, 100% employer-paid vision and life insurance, a monthly phone stipend, and an annual end of year bonus of 5% of your annual salary which can be used towards a retirement fund. 

How to Apply

Submit a resume and cover letter to karina@ctul.net using the subject line “CTUL
Deputy Director
.” Position open until filled. 

CTUL is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applications from people of color as well as persons with disabilities, LGBTQ individuals, women, immigrants, folks from working class backgrounds, and formerly incarcerated people.

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
Chief Executive Officer

Location: San Antonio, TX (relocation supported) · National travel Reports to: Board of Directors Type: Full-time, executive Compensation: Up to $2,000,000+ base, plus significant equity

The vision

We are building the first national brand in pediatric medical day care — a platform of hundreds of Prescribed Pediatric Extended Care Centers across the United States.

Today there are roughly 180 licensed centers in the entire country, operating in 15 states. Texas alone has the demand to support more than 40. Extrapolated across the states that already have a benefit, the category should be a thousand centers or more. It has stayed small because reimbursement policy suppressed it, not because families didn't need it — and that constraint is now being removed state by state.

Nobody has built the national brand. The category is still single sites and small regional operators — no consistent standard of care, no recognized name, no platform at scale.

That is what we intend to be. Hundreds of centers, one brand, one clinical standard, one operating model — and the real estate underneath all of it.

About the opportunity

A Prescribed Pediatric Extended Care Center (PPECC) is a licensed medical day center for children with complex medical needs — ventilator dependence, tracheostomies, feeding tubes, seizure protocols. Skilled pediatric nursing, on-site therapy, developmental programming and supervised transport, reimbursed by Medicaid. It costs the state less than extended hospitalization and less than round-the-clock home nursing, and it allows a parent who left the workforce to return to it.

Our capital structure, real estate strategy and site pipeline are in place. Our first center is in development in a fully built out building in San Antonio with the potential to build additional centers on the same land. We are hiring the CEO who will open it, prove the model, and scale it nationally.
This is a pre-launch role. No centers are currently operating. The person who takes this seat builds the platform from the first location.
How this is structured — and what we already have

Our board is led by a former Assistant Secretary of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services — direct experience with how federal coverage policy is made, in a category defined by it.

The platform operates as two companies. A property company owns the real estate under every center. An operating company runs the clinical business and leases from it. Both accrue to the same ownership group.
The real estate side is solved. Our partner on the property company manages approximately $500 million in assets under management, with deep development and property management experience directly relevant to this asset class. They handle site acquisition, entitlement, ground-up development, construction management, financing and property management across the portfolio. Not a gap we are hiring against — though if you have this expertise, it's a plus.

The operating side is what we are hiring for. Licensure, clinical leadership, staffing, referral development, revenue cycle, census, quality, and the multi-site playbook that turns one center into hundreds. This role owns it.

A CEO who can operate across both is ideal — someone who understands how site selection drives census, how the lease supports the capital stack, and how the real estate compounds alongside the operating business. But we are hiring for operating depth first. We will not trade clinical and multi-site operating excellence for real estate fluency, because we already have the second.

Must-have skills
Non-negotiable. A candidate strong across these is one we want to speak with, whatever their title.
De novo site opening in a licensed healthcare setting. You have personally taken facilities from licensure application through architectural plan review, life safety inspection and state survey to first patient day. Not overseen a footprint someone else built — opened them. You know where the schedule slips and what it costs.
Clinical workforce building in a shortage market. Pediatric nursing supply is the binding constraint — not capital, not real estate, not demand. You have built a nursing pipeline, hired and retained qualified clinical directors, managed turnover in a competitive labor market, and know your own retention numbers without having to look them up.
Regulatory, licensure and survey command. You have owned the relationship with a state licensing authority. You have been through inspections, received deficiencies, written and executed plans of correction, and built compliance infrastructure that holds up across multiple sites and eventually multiple states.
Government-payer revenue cycle. You have carried a P&L that was predominantly Medicaid or Medicare. You understand managed care contracting, prior authorization, medical necessity documentation, denial management and appeals — and you understand that in this category the denial rate is a leadership metric, not a billing metric.
Census and referral development. You have built demand from zero. You know how to earn the confidence of hospital discharge planners, complex-care physicians and specialty clinics, and you know these relationships are personal, slow to build, and the largest determinant of whether a center reaches breakeven on schedule.
Multi-site scaling — not multi-site management. You have taken an operating model from one location to many and know precisely what breaks between site three and site ten. You have built the centralized layer — billing, EMR, HR, compliance, quality — that makes unit economics improve with scale rather than degrade.
Full P&L ownership and unit economics fluency. You can speak to census ramp curves, contribution margin per patient, breakeven census and the working capital a ramp consumes. You have made the call on when a location is underperforming and what to do about it.
Executive team building. You have hired the people who ran your sites and your functions, and you can name both the hires that worked and the ones that didn't.
Clinical quality and risk in a medically fragile population. These are children on ventilators and feeding tubes. You bring genuine seriousness about clinical governance, incident management and family trust, and you understand that a single serious event can end a brand.

Ideal additions

Not required — what makes a candidate exceptional rather than merely qualified.
Real estate literacy. Site selection against demand and drive-time catchment, lease structuring, an understanding of how an operating tenant's credit supports property financing, and the ability to work as a peer with the property company rather than a tenant of it.
Prior CEO, board or chairman experience — or a clear appetite for it.
Multi-state licensure experience, including entering a state with no prior footprint.
M&A and integration. Acquiring regional operators will be part of the national build.
Pediatric or medically complex population experience.
Policy and payer engagement at state level.

Note on title. Prior CEO experience is not required. Some of the strongest platform builders were the second or third in command — the COO who opened the sites, the CFO whose model made scaling survivable, the commercial leader who filled them. If you did the building and someone else held the title, you are exactly who we want to hear from — and if you held the CEO title, that works too.

Compensation and equity
Base salary — up to $2,000,000+, commensurate with experience and track record. We are prepared to pay at the top of the market for the right operator.
Annual performance bonus of 50–100% of base, tied to centers opened, census performance, survey outcomes and clinical quality.

Significant equity — in both companies. The CEO participates in the operating company and the property company.

Operating company equity. A meaningful founder-level ownership position, not a management-pool allocation.

Property company participation. A direct stake in the real estate portfolio. Every center is owned rather than leased, and across hundreds of locations that portfolio becomes an asset base in its own right. Operating executives are rarely offered a position in the real estate they generate the credit for. This one is.
Stock option grants available in addition to direct equity, with strike set at current valuation ahead of the first center opening.

Vesting over four to five years, milestone-weighted to centers opened and stabilized.
Acceleration on change of control.

Benefits. Full medical, dental and vision · relocation assistance · executive severance · change-of-control protections.

We would rather pay in ownership than in salary — and we are prepared to do both.

What you will do

Phase one — prove it
Take the launch center from licensure application through Life Safety Code survey to first patient day
Recruit and lead the clinical leadership team, including the nursing director required for licensure
Build referral relationships with children's hospitals, discharge planning, complex-care pediatrics and home health agencies
Establish billing, prior authorization and denial management operations with Medicaid managed care organizations
Drive census from opening to stabilization

Phase two — make it repeatable
Convert the launch center into a documented, transferable opening playbook
Open and stabilize centers.
Build the centralized services layer so that unit economics improve rather than degrade with scale
Recruit and lead the executive team

Phase three — build the brand
Lead multi-state expansion, including acquisition of regional operators where that is the faster path
Establish the clinical standard and brand identity that defines the category nationally
Own the full P&L and report to the board

jobs.title: STScI Deputy Director
jobs.description:
The Space Telescope Science Institute advances humanity’s exploration of the universe by ensuring open access to frontier space observatories and comprehensive scientific data services. STScI is the operational home of the Hubble Space Telescope, the James Webb Space Telescope, and the upcoming Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, along with ever-growing data archives and public outreach. These missions are guided by a world-class staff of astronomers who support all areas of mission activity and conduct their own self-guided independent research. With continued scientific success of both the Hubble and the James Webb Space Telescopes, STScI begins a new and exciting multi-mission era that will grow again with Roman and future strategic missions (e.g., the Habitable Worlds Observatory).

We are seeking an experienced leader to serve as the STScI Deputy Director, a member of the STScI executive leadership team reporting to the STScI Director. This role is focused primarily on ensuring effective internal operations of STScI and our missions, enabling strategic multi-mission innovations, and maintaining positive working culture for all staff. The Deputy Director is responsible multi-mission operational and tactical leadership, issue management, and conflict resolution to assist in achieving STScI’s objectives. The Deputy Director works with the STScI Mission Heads and Associate Directors to align execution across the Institute’s multiple missions toward shared Institute strategic objectives. The Deputy also supports the Director in engagement with key external stakeholders and the scientific community to demonstrate the value of STScI and the progress in the execution of our missions.

The ideal candidate has extensive experience leading operations of a large-scale scientific facility. Demonstrated strong leadership, excellent communication skills, effective project management and execution, adaptability, and a strong commitment to service are required for success in this role. The candidate will have earned PhD in astronomy or astrophysics and a history of research excellence or equivalent in a field relevant to STScI’s missions.

The Space Telescope Science Institute is operated by the Association of Universities for Research in Astronomy (AURA) and is based on the Johns Hopkins University Homewood campus in Baltimore, Maryland. This position can support hybrid work, with at least three days on site per week
. Candidates must reside in or be willing to relocate to our local market. (MD, DE, VA, PA, DC & WV). Some travel is required.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities

Internal Management and Operations:

Oversees the STScI Leadership Team; coordinates the leadership engagement and communications across Mission Offices and Divisions
High-level oversight and line-management of assigned Mission Offices and the Instruments Division
Provides cross-mission operational coordination, including strategic innovations, resource allocation, and STScI-wide priority setting
Lead long-range multi-mission workforce planning at enterprise level
Identify and support new growth opportunities and efficiency gains in operations
Ensures science-first focus on multi-mission operational outcomes

Team Leadership

Works with the Director, Chief of Staff, and Associate Directors to set STScI-wide strategic objectives and strategic plans
Lead STScI-wide projects and strategic initiatives on behalf of the Director’s Office, as assigned
Leads the staff engagement survey, awards committees, and supporting activities to monitor and foster strong positive working culture and employee morale
Supports the Director’s Office engagement with the STScI scientific staff on matters relating to research support, promotions, and internal research environment.

Stakeholder Engagement

High-level oversight and line-management of the Office of Public Outreach Head
Represents STScI within the AURA leadership and supports cross-AURA initiatives as needed.
Leads preparations for external oversight committee meetings and visiting committees, including Board approvals for new proposals and contracts.
Represents STScI and the Director at user committee meetings and the scientific meetings.
Maintains and supports strong working relationships with NASA and ESA leadership and other partners.
Engages with the scientific community on future of space-based astronomy, including National Academy recommendations and the future of STScI’s missions

Executive Support

Discharges the duties of the Director in the event of her absence, according to STScI policy
Leadership and assumption of other duties as assigned by the Director

The annual salary range for this title is $275,000 - $315,000. The posted salary range represents a general guideline; however, STScI considers a number of factors when determining base salary offers, such as internal pay equity, the scope and responsibilities of the position, the candidate's experience, education, and skill, and current market conditions.

Candidates should submit a cover letter, complete CV, and list of professional references as a single pdf. The cover letter should address the candidate’s excitement about STScI’s missions, explain how their experience provides the background and skills to lead a complex science organization, and share thoughts about the changing landscape and future direction of astrophysics. Note that references will not be contacted until the final stage of the process. Complete applications received by July 27th
will receive full consideration. Reference #: HRC0015416

Please ONLY use this link to apply
: https://stsci.slideroom.com/#/permalink/program/89413

We offer an excellent and comprehensive benefits package, generous retirement, work life balance and a stimulating and diverse work environment. Explore our benefits: http://www.stsci.edu/opportunities/benefits

Individuals needing assistance with the employment process can contact us at careers@stsci.edu.

jobs.description:
KLR Executive Search Group is proud to partner with a premier New England full-service law firm to identify an experienced Chief Operating Officer (COO) to lead daily business operations and administrative functions. Based in New Hampshire, with additional offices across New Hampshire and Massachusetts, the firm has built a strong regional reputation and continues to expand its presence across key markets. The COO will work closely with firm leadership to strengthen operational infrastructure, improve systems and processes, enhance financial reporting and business analytics, support technology implementation, and drive cross-office alignment. This is a key leadership opportunity created by the planned retirement of the firm’s current CEO/Executive Director. Ideal candidates will bring strong operational leadership experience within a law firm, professional services firm, or similarly complex organization, along with the ability to manage change, build consensus, and support continued financial success and growth.

Core Responsibilities
:
Operational Leadership

Oversee firm-wide daily operations across all offices, ensuring coordination, communication, consistency, and alignment across the firm’s New England footprint.
Serve as a strategic partner to firm leadership, practice group leaders, department heads, and administrative teams on operational priorities, staffing, resources, and growth initiatives.
Lead administrative infrastructure across Finance, HR, IT, Facilities, Operations, and related business functions while supporting succession planning, recruiting, retention, compensation, and a collaborative team-first culture.

Technology & Process Innovation

Lead business process optimization initiatives across departments and offices, improving workflows, documentation, internal coordination, service delivery, and overall efficiency.
Support the implementation and optimization of accounting systems, HRIS tools, AI solutions, and other technologies while partnering with IT, Finance, HR, and administrative leaders to strengthen systems integration, data quality, reporting, and best practices.

Financial Management and Resource Allocation

Partner with Finance and firm leadership to strengthen financial reporting, business analytics, budgeting, forecasting, and performance tracking.
Use financial and operational data to support informed decision-making across staffing, systems, infrastructure, real estate, and office planning.
Align resources with short-, mid-, and long-term operational goals while identifying opportunities to improve efficiency, manage costs, and support thoughtful investment.

Risk and Compliance

Support firm leadership in maintaining clear policies, consistent procedures, sound administrative practices, and strong internal controls across offices and departments.
Partner with Finance, HR, IT, and other leaders to identify operational risks and implement practical solutions, ensuring new systems and processes are rolled out with appropriate attention to compliance, training, change management, transparency, and accountability.

Qualifications :

Executive operational leadership experience within a complex organization, ideally a law firm, professional services firm, or comparable business environment.
Experience overseeing or partnering with Finance, HR, IT, Facilities, Operations, and other administrative functions.
Proven ability to lead operational improvements, systems implementations, process optimization, change management, financial analysis, and business reporting initiatives.
Strong communication, relationship-building, and leadership skills, with the ability to earn credibility in a consensus-driven, team-first environment.
This role is based in New Hampshire
, with oversight across multiple offices in New Hampshire and Massachusetts. Local or relocation candidates are preferred.

jobs.title: Vice President of Development
jobs.description:
About Us

The Climate Solutions Fund (CSF) is a cutting-edge climate and energy advocacy organization working to build bipartisan support for policies essential to achieving decarbonization at scale and speed.

We have a fundamental belief that without bipartisanship, the level of progress needed to address America's many energy and environmental challenges cannot be achieved. Few things in this realm are more important. We are working directly to fill that gap.

We have a passionate, mission-driven, and bipartisan team. As we continue to grow and scale our impact, we are seeking motivated early- to mid-career leaders to join us in this vital mission.

The Role:

CSF is looking for a Vice President of Development to lead fundraising, including and especially with the rapidly evolving world of AI and tech. You will own the strategy, professionalize and expand the funder pipeline, and work directly alongside CSF's Co-Founders and executive team to translate our mission into resources we need to rapidly grow.

Location
: Remote, with preference for New York, NY, Washington, DC, or San Francisco, CA
Compensation
: $120,000–$150,000 annually, plus a benefits package including health, dental, and vision insurance, flexible PTO, paid parental leave, and retirement contribution
Employment Type
: Full-Time, Exempt
Reports To
: President

Your Responsibilities:

You will build and own CSF's fundraising strategy — setting annual revenue targets, identifying prospects, running cultivation, managing solicitations, and stewarding relationships through the full cycle. Specifically, you will:

- Develop and manage a portfolio of major donors, family offices, foundations, and corporate partners, with a focus on six-figure gifts
- Draft compelling funding proposals, grant applications, partnership briefs, and donor correspondence that translate CSF's policy work into a clear case for investment, owning the funding/grant cycles and deadlines
- Build and maintain the infrastructure that keeps the operation running cleanly — tracking all donor activity, pipeline status, and giving history
- Report regularly to the President on fundraising metrics, pipeline health, and progress against goals
- Manage grant tracking, gift processing, and acknowledgment functions
- As CSF scales, help define the team and infrastructure the organization will need to grow — with the expectation that this role may eventually lead a small team

Who We’re Looking For:

- Five or more years of experience in development, fundraising (non-profit or startup), or external affairs, and a track record of personally cultivating and closing major gifts
- You've worked with high-net-worth individuals, private investors, family offices, or foundations — ideally with some exposure to the technology or venture world, or a demonstrated ability to build new networks quickly
- You write with precision and persuasion
- You manage a complex pipeline without losing threads
- You're low ego and relationship-first in everything you do
- Familiarity with Salesforce or a comparable CRM is expected
- Experience with fundraising strategy, grant research tools, or email marketing platforms is a plus
- Most importantly: you're genuinely excited about our mission — and you want to be part of an organization that's disrupting the status quo and paving a new path forward

Why CSF:

This is not a role inside a legacy institution. CSF is building something new in one of the most important gaps in America's energy and environmental space. We're constantly innovating, unearthing and implementing critical insights, and now we need focused help to scale this work at such a pivotal moment in U.S. history.

How to Apply

Please submit your application, including your resume/CV and a cover letter, to hiring@climatesolutionsfund.org. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.
