jobs.title: Development Director* and Team Lead, School of Business
jobs.description:
Description

Development Director* and Team Lead - School of Business

Department: Lawrence Development

Reports to: Assistant Vice President for Development

FLSA Status: Exempt

The anticipated salary for this position starts at $124,100+ for the Development Director role and ranges up to $136,500+ for the Senior Development Director role. The final pay will be determined based on several factors, including qualifications, experience, education, and years within the job or industry, with a strong emphasis on maintaining internal equity.

Note: This position will be filled at the Development Director or Sr. Development Director level, commensurate with the successful candidate’s experience and qualifications.

Development Director – At least five years of professional experience in higher education/healthcare fundraising. This may be substituted for seven or more years or professional experience in an area with strong similarities to development work. Major gift experience required. Must have success managing a diverse portfolio.

Sr. Development Director - At least ten years of professional experience in higher education/healthcare fundraising. This may be substituted for ten or more years of professional experience in an area with strong similarities to development work. Major gift experience required. Must have success managing a diverse portfolio.

Who We Are And What We’re About

KU Endowment is an independent, nonprofit organization and the official fundraising foundation for the University of Kansas. We partner with donors in providing philanthropic support to build a greater KU.

As an employee of KU Endowment, you’ll witness firsthand the power of our donors’ generosity. You will join a talented and fun group of people who are inspired to help KU through the support of loyal alumni and friends. This support fuels excellence, enabling students and faculty to develop innovative programs and research that distinguish the university. We connect the philanthropic passions of donors with the needs of students, faculty and programs – and everyone benefits.

People are engaged. Purpose is ignited. Lives are changed.

What We Are Looking For

Full-time professional fundraising position responsible for the organization, implementation, and direction of a comprehensive development program for raising private gifts to The Kansas University Endowment Association in support of the School of Business and the University of Kansas. Provides assistance to appropriate University personnel and KU Endowment staff in the coordination, planning, and implementation of fundraising activities of the School of Business.

The individual in this position is expected to embrace KU Endowment’s stated core values – passion for KU, partnership with donors, perpetual support for KU, and a people-centered approach – and demonstrate support for them through professional interactions and activities.

Hybrid Work Environment

Following the initial training period, this position offers a hybrid work schedule, with the flexibility to work remotely for part of the week. However, the role requires multiple days per week on-site at our Lawrence, KS office to ensure effective collaboration, team engagement, and alignment with organizational goals.

What’s a typical day look like?

Directs programs for identification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of prospective and current major gift donors to the School of Business and the University of Kansas working with the dean, department chairs, KU Endowment officers and professional staff, University administrators, faculty, and volunteer leaders.
Solicits major gifts ($50,000+), both outright and deferred, in support of the stated needs of the School of Business.
Crafts and deploys a strategic operational plan – develops and implements both annual and long-range goals, objectives, and strategies to maximize private support for the School of Business, consistent with the overall plan of KU Endowment. As a team lead, candidates will demonstrate strong, seasoned, professional experience.
Coordinates preparation and development of major gift proposals to School of Business prospects for presentation by the University Chancellor, the Dean of the School, KU Endowment staff, and volunteer leaders.
Supervises the shared development assistant who provides administrative support for this constituent area.
Manages, monitors and mentors two other professional fundraisers supporting the School of Business that are focused on strategic identification of prospects and timely and consistent movement through management stages (qualification, cultivation, solicitation and stewardship).
Position requires an extensive amount of time out of the office traveling to meet development goals, as well as attendance at events occurring outside of traditional business hours.
Collaborate with Development Team Leads to identify and implement best practices, align strategies, and support clear communication across the broader Development team.
Regular and reliable attendance is essential for this position.
Performs such other duties as may be assigned from time to time by the Vice President for Development and other senior development staff.

Requirements

What’s required to be considered:

Bachelor’s degree required.
A minimum of five years of successful professional experience in major gift fundraising is required. Higher education fundraising experience is preferred.
Prior management experience is also preferred, particularly experience coaching, mentoring, or supporting the professional growth of individual team members in a fundraising environment.
Excellent written, oral, and communication skills and the ability to work independently, as well as part of a team, are essential.
Must possess the ability to independently initiate, organize and follow through with fundraising strategies.
Must be able to work with minimal supervision and exercise excellent judgement.
Adept at using social media, text, phone, and video conferencing.
Strong work ethic with a desire to continue to learn.
Entrepreneurial spirit and fearless approach to portfolio building and relationship development.
Willingness to step outside one’s comfort zone and embrace challenges.
Excellent organizational skills.
Exceptional interpersonal skills and readiness to engage donors and alumni.
Skilled at navigating and using databases, familiarity with CRM systems, and entering contact reports.
Ability to establish and maintain professional and effective working relationships inside and outside KU Endowment.
Ability to adapt to new procedures and technology.

What separates the best from the rest?

Self-Motivated: You’re a go-getter who takes initiative and drives projects forward with enthusiasm and determination.
Collaborative: You shine in a team setting. Whether with your colleagues, prospective donors, or campus partners, you’re always finding ways to combine efforts and maximize outcomes.
Master Communicator: You have a knack for articulating compelling stories and connecting with donors on a deep level.
Resilient: You stay positive and persistent, turning challenges into opportunities and never losing sight of the mission.
Unwavering Integrity: You build trust through honesty, transparency, and the highest ethical standards.
Growth Mindset: You embrace challenges, see setbacks as learning opportunities and consistently strive to grow in personal and professional endeavors.

Physical Requirements

A valid driver’s license.
Must have access to reliable transportation to travel between multiple locations for training, donor meetings, networking, events and/or work-related activities.
Must be able to respond quickly and effectively to directions and safety hazards.
This job operates in a professional office environment. This role routinely uses standard office productivity machinery such as computers, keyboards and a mouse, laptops, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets and fax machines.
This is largely a sedentary role; however, some filing is required. This would require the ability to move files, open filing cabinets and bend or stand as necessary.
The ability to observe details at close range (within a few feet of the observer).
The person in this position frequently communicates with donors, colleagues, students, and general public. Must be able to communicate with others effectively.
Physical effort/lifting, such as up to 40 pounds.
Often travel requirements can include the following, evenings, weekends, and out of town overnights as a regular part of the job.

KU Endowment is committed to providing reasonable accommodation to qualified individuals to enable them to perform the essential functions of the job as regulated by ADA. If you require accommodation for any part of the application process or during employment, please contact humanresources@kuendowment.org to discuss your needs.

We’ve Got You Covered

KU Endowment offers a competitive salary with an excellent benefit package including:
100% employer-paid pension plan
Health, dental, and vision insurance
401(k) and Roth 401(k) matching program
Section 125 flexible benefits
Generous paid leave including holidays, personal and sick leave
Volunteer and community engagement opportunities
Paid Parental Leave
Life, AD&D, short- and long-term disability insurance
Legal & ID Theft protection
Critical Illness
Accident and Hospital Indemnity insurance
KU education assistance
Free parking

Sounds good, right?
What’s next?

Please include your resume and cover letter when applying for this position on our website at https://www.kuendowment.org/careers.

Questions Regarding This Vacancy May Be Addressed To

Human Resources

KU Endowment

PO Box 928

Lawrence, KS 66044-0928

Email: humanresources@kuendowment.org

Review of applications begins immediately and continues until the position is filled. Because of the sensitive nature of KU Endowment business and the importance of our reputation with donors, KU Endowment conducts a comprehensive review of applicants. By submitting your application, you authorize us to conduct reference checks and a review of available public information. Employment is contingent upon background and reference checks that KU Endowment finds acceptable.

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
CDEL Director of Development

The Center for Disability & Elder Law (CDEL) provides free legal services to low-income older adults and persons with disabilities.  CDEL’s clients, individuals often marginalized by age, disability and income, may have difficulty accessing the legal system. CDEL strives to provide access to the legal system to assist our clients in asserting their legal rights and living better lives.

CDEL is seeking an accomplished and visionary Director of Development to help drive the continued growth and impact of our organization as we expand access to justice for older adults and people with disabilities. This is an exceptional opportunity for a strategic, relationship-driven leader who is energized by building, creating and growing meaningful partnerships, creating new opportunities and driving organizational growth. Working in close partnership with the Executive Director and CDEL’s Board, the Director of Development will lead the evolution of CDEL's fundraising program. This is more than a fundraising role—it's an opportunity to design strategy, cultivate meaningful partnerships, elevate CDEL's profile throughout Chicagoland and build the resources necessary to expand access to justice for older adults and people with disabilities.

We are looking for a leader who combines creativity with execution, sees possibilities where others see obstacles and is excited by the opportunity to build something lasting. The successful candidate will be an entrepreneurial thinker, an exceptional relationship builder and a persuasive communicator who inspires others to invest in CDEL's mission. If you are motivated by purpose, inspired by our mission, thrive in a collaborative leadership environment and want the opportunity to leave a lasting legacy, we invite you to join us.

This is a hybrid position, with the expectation of being in the office 2-3 days per week. Ability to work some evenings and weekends as needed for donor stewardship activities and special events is required.

Please apply through our applicant tracking portal at: 
https://disability.bamboohr.com/careers/86

Responsibilities

Provides leadership and development of planning, creating and executing annual fundraising programs and activities. The programs and activities will include foundation, corporate, government, individual donor solicitation and cultivation, major gifts, planned giving and special events.

Creates and implements long-range strategic development plan, including comprehensive detailed fundraising plan with calendar.

Oversees and participates in comprehensive Major Gifts program, including managing donor and prospective donor relationships through a comprehensive stewardship program of introduction, cultivation and solicitation.

Works with the Executive Director, Board of Directors and staff to ensure that Development Plan supports CDEL’s strategic plan and common objectives.

Generates, with appropriate staff, donor/prospect solicitation material and correspondence.

Supervises CDEL’s Development Associate, including overseeing and participating in in the creation of marketing/communications messages and deliverables such as newsletters, social media and website that inform the public, donors and supporters of CDEL’s activities, progress and needs.

Meets fundraising goals set by the Fundraising Committee, the Board of Directors and the Executive Director.

Manages CDEL’s grant calendar and works with CDEL’s grant writing consultant and appropriate staff to ensure that grant applications and reports are submitted on a timely basis.

Plans and coordinates funder/donor site visits to compellingly communicate CDEL’s programs and services.

Participates in key decisions as a member of the senior management team.

Attends all CDEL fundraising and cultivation events.

Qualifications

At least six years of prior development experience.

A minimum of three years of experience in a frontline development position with a proven track record in soliciting and closing major gifts.

Experience working with external stakeholders required. Demonstrated experience working with volunteer leaders a plus.

Experience in developing compelling marketing/communications messaging and the type of materials that support the overall fundraising goals of CDEL.

Prior experience administering government and foundation grants.

Demonstrated commitment to CDEL’s client base, including working with older adults and people with disabilities.

Confident and authentic presentation skills and ability to represent CDEL in a variety of settings including large group presentations and individual meetings.

Ability to engage clearly with CDEL staff and stakeholders through excellent written and oral communication skills.

Ability to prioritize multiple projects and complete assignments in a timely manner, including responding to unexpected events.

Work collaboratively and communicate transparently with other staff members.

Prior supervisor experience required.

Salary and Benefits

Salary range: $90K-$110K depending on experience. CDEL is open to discussion with candidates based on proven track record and experience. Full-time position. CDEL offers a comprehensive benefits package including health/dental/vision insurance, flexible spending accounts, 401(k), life insurance and short and long-term disability insurance. CDEL also has a generous personal time off policy. 

About the organization

The Center for Disability & Elder Law (CDEL) is a dynamic 501(c)(3) legal aid organization dedicated to empowering low-income older adults and persons with disabilities in Chicago and Cook County through pro bono legal assistance. Founded by the Chicago Bar Association in 1982, CDEL serves more than 3,000 clients annually, delivering access to justice and improving lives.

With a talented in-house team and a network of hundreds of volunteer attorneys, students, and paralegals, CDEL focuses on resolving legal challenges in four key practice areas: end-of-life planning, adult guardianship, housing and consumer issues. Through innovative programs and passionate advocacy, CDEL ensures that legal solutions are within reach for thousands of individuals every year, enabling them to lead their lives with dignity and security.

CDEL is an equal opportunity employer. We are a welcoming community that engages, encourages and empowers those who are members of underrepresented communities. We are a dedicated advocate for our clients who hold many diverse identities and experiences, across race, color, sex, age, religion, class, disability, national origin, ancestry, sexual orientation, marital status, parental status, military discharge, source of income, housing status and gender identity. We believe that these communities must be centered in the work we do. Hence, we strongly encourage applications from people with these identities or who are members of other marginalized and underrepresented communities to apply for this position. We also encourage you to read our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement available on our website.

Position is open immediately and will be closed when filled. Applications received by August 15, 2026 will be given preference.

Please apply through our applicant tracking portal at: 
https://disability.bamboohr.com/careers/86

jobs.title: Director of Advancement Services
jobs.description:
The Shipley School seeks a fundraising data professional with 2 to 3 years of experience to manage a Raiser's Edge NXT database, prospect research, and stewardship in the Alumni and Development office. The role is a full-time salaried and onsite position with one direct report. Experience with Raiser's Edge NXT is strongly preferred.Working as a member of the Development Office and reporting to the Director of Development, the Director of Advancement Services supports the fundraising work of the Development Office, maintaining close working relationships with constituent-based gift officers, the Development Administrative Coordinator, and the Directors of Data Operations and Finance. The position supervises the Advancement Services Coordinator. The Director is responsible for three major program areas: database management, prospect research and management, and stewardship support for all constituencies.

Key Responsibilities

Database Management & Reporting:

Manage the Raiser's Edge CRM database to ensure the integrity and accuracy of information entered in the database and that all protocols have been followed. Authorize and record security permissions.
In collaboration with various constituents, oversee the continued evolution or enhancement and implementation of policies, such as gift acceptance, database and gift entry, and gift counting and reporting.
Develop and maintain procedures for use of Raiser's Edge database. Train staff on policies and processes, as well as the use of the database. Update and maintain a written procedures manual to ensure data processing and business continuity. Review the procedures manual with the Director of Development on an annual basis.
Stay informed of the latest product updates from Blackbaud and other vendors in order to implement new data processing and other procedures as needed to keep systems and processes up to date.
Serve as the integration liaison between Raiser's Edge and onCore and work closely with Director of Data Operations to ensure ongoing communication and smooth interface between the two databases. This includes managing ongoing database maintenance and procedures to ensure accuracy, consistency, and integrity.
Supervise the Advancement Services Coordinator, ensuring the accurate and timely processing and acknowledgment of gifts, pledges, pledge payments, matching claims, memorial gifts, in honor gifts, and other contributions to Shipley. Monitor the timeliness and efficiency of processing and ensure that all corresponding links in the database are updated and maintained as gifts are processed. Record links may include but will not be limited to spouses and other family members; other alumni and friends; corporations/foundations to employees/owners, etc.
Fulfill data requests for the Head of School, the Assistant Head of School for Community Engagement, the Director of Development, the Development Office, and the Communications & Marketing Office by querying and exporting information from Raiser's Edge, as well as produce reports as requested, including monthly gift reports for the Business Office and annual reporting for NAIS.
Oversee the generation of donor lists for annual Gratitude Report and ensure their accuracy.
Manage summer database changes and coordinate changes with gift officers.

Prospect Research & Management

Identify and conduct ongoing research of major prospects and donors.
Write and maintain major prospect and donor profiles.
Process Call Reports written by a prospect's Prospect Manager (PM). This includes gathering new information on prospect, updating prospect profile, doing more research if necessary, and filing documentation in Raiser's Edge.
Generate and analyze Pipeline reports and Prospect Manager reports. Add alerts on database records of Pipeline prospects.
With the Director of Development, perform a periodic review of the Moves Management System and identify and recommend a course of action for improvement.
Co-lead monthly Prospect Managers meetings with the Director of Development.

Stewardship

Collaborate with gift officers to devise and execute annual personalized stewardship plans for all donor constituencies. Maintain and ensure adherence to a set schedule and proactively gather examples of philanthropic impact across campus.
Manage ongoing stewardship of constituents connected to named endowment funds, coordinating efforts with PMs, where applicable. This includes gathering student scholarship recipient information, student-authored letters to donors, and endowment values, and incorporating the information into donor correspondence.
Manage ongoing stewardship of constituents connected to student awards, coordinating efforts with PMs, where applicable. This includes invitations to yearly awards ceremonies and follow-up correspondence with information about award recipients.
Collaborate with PMs in devising and implementing stewardship strategies for Centennial Society members and donors connected to restricted operating funds.
Maintain gift acknowledgement letter templates.
Manage pledge reminder process.
Maintain an inventory of donor recognition signage across campus and update annually as necessary. Coordinate signage generation on an ongoing basis.

General Responsibilities

Manage in coordination with the Director of Data Operations relationships with vendors, such as providers of database, online giving, and prospect research services. Manage subscriptions and conduct an annual review to determine potential renewals, cancellations, or alternatives.
Process all department invoices and check requests. Support reconciliation of budget sections with the Business Office.
Manage end of year financial reconciliation with Business Office and auditors.

jobs.title: Deputy Director-Recreation Services
jobs.description:
Deputy Director- Recreation Services

Join our Great City

The City of Alexandria is in Northern Virginia bordered by the District of Columbia (Potomac River), Arlington, and Fairfax counties. Once within the original boundary stones that demarked the area of the Nation’s Capital, Alexandria is a part of the greater Washington, D.C. metropolitan area. With a population of approximately 157,600 Alexandria is the sixth largest city in the Commonwealth of Virginia. Unique and historic, Alexandria is a place that is experiencing substantial redevelopment appeal and business growth opportunities because of a high market demand for urban living in a quality environment. About one-quarter of the City’s 15.47 square miles has been designated as a national or local historic district.

The City of Alexandria is an independent City with no county affiliation and derives its governing authority from a Charter granted by the Virginia General Assembly. Virginia is a Dillon Rule state, allowing local governments only the authority granted to them by the Virginia Constitution or by the Virginia General Assembly in the City Charter or in general law. City Council is the governing body, which formulates policies for the administration of the City. The mayor is chosen on a separate ballot and presides over sessions of the Council. If you are interested in working for the vibrant City of Alexandria, we invite qualified candidates to apply for our Deputy Director Recreation Services for Recreation, Parks and Cultural Activities (RPCA).

A Message from the Director

Thank you for your interest in joining the City of Alexandria’s Department of Recreation, Parks, and Cultural Activities.

This is more than a leadership position—it’s an opportunity to shape the future of one of the nation’s premier park and recreation systems and make a lasting impact on the lives of more than 160,000 residents.

As our next Deputy Director of Recreation Services
, you will serve as a trusted member of the Executive Leadership Team, leading a diverse portfolio that includes recreation centers, aquatics, youth and adult programming, out-of-school time services, therapeutic recreation, customer experience, and community partnerships. You will help define the vision, set the standard for operational excellence, and lead a talented team dedicated to delivering exceptional public service.

We are looking for an accomplished executive who is both strategic and people-centered, someone who inspires teams, embraces innovation, drives accountability, and is passionate about building inclusive, welcoming communities where everyone has the opportunity to thrive. This role requires a leader who is comfortable navigating complex challenges, developing future leaders, and turning bold ideas into measurable results.

At RPCA, we believe parks, recreation, and cultural activities are essential public services that strengthen neighborhoods, improve health and wellness, support youth and families, and enhance quality of life. We are building a high-performing organization focused on innovation, customer experience, operational excellence, and continuous improvement, and we’re looking for a leader who wants to help shape that future.

If you’re looking for an opportunity to make a meaningful difference, lead at the executive level, and leave a legacy in a nationally recognized community, I encourage you to apply.

I look forward to welcoming our next Deputy Director and partnering with you to build the future of recreation in Alexandria.

Hashim T. Taylor

Director

Department of Recreation, Parks, and Cultural Activities

City of Alexandria

About Us

The City of Alexandria’s Department of Recreation, Parks & Cultural Activities (RPCA) plays a vital role in shaping the quality of life, health, and character of our community. With more than 900 acres of parkland, 100 parks and open spaces, and over 30 recreation, nature, and cultural facilities, RPCA delivers programs and services that connect people to each other and to the world around them.

From Old Town’s Waterfront Park to Ben Brenman Park, Chinquapin Park Recreation Center & Aquatics Facility, Patrick Henry Recreation Center, and the Torpedo Factory Art Center, our facilities and programs reflect the City’s commitment to inclusion, creativity, and service. RPCA’s employees help ensure that every experience, whether a community event, park visit, or recreation program, is safe, welcoming, and enriching for all. The Department is organized into four core operating divisions: Recreation Services, Park Operations, Cultural Activities, and Leadership & Management.

The Department’s mission- We create meaningful experiences that inspire community, foster creativity, and enhance quality of life for everyone.
The Department’s vision– The Department of Recreation, Parks & Cultural Activities envisions Alexandria as a vibrant, connected, and thriving community where every resident feels a sense of belonging. Through safe, inclusive and innovative programs and spaces, we strengthen well-being, celebrate diversity, and create opportunities for joy and discovery. Guided by equity and excellence, we are dedicated to enhancing the quality of life for all who live, work and play in our city.

The Deputy Director

Provide strategic leadership for Recreation Services, including aquatics, recreation centers, youth and adult programming, therapeutic recreation, out-of-school time programs, customer service, and community engagement.
Advance a culture of exceptional customer service by implementing department-wide customer service standards, strengthening the customer experience, and centralizing customer service and registration functions.
Develop and implement innovative recreation programs, services, and operational strategies that respond to evolving community needs and expand access to high-quality recreational opportunities.
Lead the implementation of a comprehensive Youth Sports Affiliate Policy that strengthens partnerships, promotes equitable access, and improves the management of athletic programming and facilities.
Strengthen operational excellence by establishing performance measures, documenting standard operating procedures, implementing data-driven decision-making, and fostering a culture of accountability and continuous improvement.
Build and develop high-performing teams by investing in leadership development, succession planning, employee engagement, and professional growth opportunities throughout the Recreation Services Division.
Manage financial resources responsibly by overseeing division budgets, identifying operational efficiencies, maximizing revenue opportunities, and ensuring fiscal stewardship.
Collaborate with City departments, Alexandria City Public Schools, community organizations, nonprofit partners, and regional agencies to enhance recreation services, expand partnerships, and improve community outcomes.
Use performance data, customer feedback, and industry best practices to evaluate programs, measure organizational impact, and guide strategic planning and service improvements.
Serve as a member of the Department’s Executive Leadership Team, helping shape the department’s strategic direction while fostering innovation, collaboration, and organizational excellence.

The Deputy Director Key Work Projects FY27-FY28

Lead the implementation of the Recreation Services Master Plan, establishing a long-term vision for recreation programs, facilities, aquatics, community centers, and equitable access to services.
Conduct a comprehensive Community Needs Assessment to evaluate current and future recreation needs, identify service gaps, and develop recommendations that respond to changing community demographics and priorities.
Modernize Recreation Operations by implementing standardized operating procedures, performance measures, and data-driven decision-making to improve consistency, accountability, and service delivery across all Recreation Services divisions.
Transform the Customer Experience through the implementation of department-wide customer service standards, customer service training, and the centralization of customer service and registration functions.
Strengthen Community Partnerships by expanding collaboration with Alexandria City Public Schools, youth sports affiliates, community organizations, nonprofit partners, and regional agencies to enhance programming, maximize shared resources, and increase access to recreation opportunities.
Develop Future Leaders and Build Organizational Capacity by implementing leadership development, succession planning, workforce development, and employee engagement initiatives that strengthen organizational culture and prepare the next generation of Recreation Services leaders.
Collaborate with other City departments on joint projects involving planning for and deployment of programs for youth and adults (Library, OHA, DCHS, Schools).

What You Should Bring

We are seeking a collaborative, innovative, and people-centered leader who is passionate about creating exceptional recreation experiences and strengthening communities through public service. The successful candidate will bring executive-level experience leading complex recreation and community services organizations, with a demonstrated ability to inspire teams, foster a high-performing organizational culture, and lead meaningful organizational change.

You should possess a proven track record of building collaborative relationships across departments, partnering with schools, community organizations, and regional stakeholders, and using data and performance measures to guide strategic decision-making, improve operational effectiveness, and enhance the customer experience. The ideal candidate is a visionary leader who embraces innovation, develops future leaders, and is committed to continuous improvement and service excellence.

Most importantly, you believe in the power of recreation to improve quality of life. You lead with integrity, accountability, and empathy, while creating welcoming, inclusive, and engaging opportunities that enrich the lives of residents of all ages and abilities. Your leadership will help shape the future of Recreation Services by delivering innovative programs, exceptional customer experiences, and lasting community impact

Minimum & Additional Requirements

Bachelors’ degree in recreation and parks or related field; seven (7) years of related experience, of which three (3) years must be at the supervisory or managerial level, in planning, coordinating, and conducting park maintenance, forestry, land management, park design, community planning, and park construction operations or equivalent combination of experience and training which provides the required knowledge, skills, and abilities.

Preferred Qualifications

Master's degree, executive municipal leadership experience, presentations to elected officials, organizational transformation, and professional certifications (CPRP, CPRE, CPM, or similar)

Notes

This position requires the successful completion of a criminal records background check and drug screening prior to employment.

Please note that the working location is: 1108 Jefferson St, Alexandria, VA 22314, USA

The City of Alexandria is an Equal Opportunity Employer and values diversity in its workforce. All qualified candidates will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, age, marital status, personal appearance, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, family responsibilities, matriculation, political affiliation, genetic information, military status, or disability.

Job Description

SALARY: Negotiable Depending on Qualifications

Under general administrative direction this position is responsible for planning, directing, managing, and overseeing the activities and operations of the Economic Development Department, including small business development, infill development, redevelopment, tax increment financing zones, economic development corporations, and industrial districts.

Essential Functions/ Job Competencies/ Physical Requirements

ESSENTIAL DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

The intent of this job description is to provide a representative summary of the major duties and responsibilities performed by employees in this job. Employees may be requested to perform job-related tasks other than those specifically presented in this description.

Oversees all operational activities of the Economic Development Department.
Formulates and administers the planning and grant writing activities for the City of Laredo. Research available grant sources and submits applications to all potential funding sources to include private foundations, local trusts, and to state and federal agencies.
Assures grant management in accordance with all federal, state, and local requirements including requirements stipulated by private foundations and/or trusts.
Directs the research, collection, and analysis of trade related data from production of reports and publications on economic status.
Supervises, plans, schedules, and assigns work to subordinates; instructs and trains in correct methods and procedures; reviews and evaluates employee's performance.
Develops and supervises the implementation of an annual operating and marketing plan, in order to promote Laredo as a destination to local, state, regional, national, and international audiences.
Directs the development and preparation of the annual departmental budget and monitors expenditures to ensure compliance with budget limitations.
Develops collateral materials including brochures, newsletters, promotional documents, advertisements, direct mail pieces and planner's guides to increase market shares for the Economic Development industry in Laredo.
Develops economic updates, reports, and publications.
Assists potential investors.
Directs the planning and coordination of promotional activities for the City of Laredo.
Promotes development operation in the City of Laredo and other foreign countries such as Mexico, China, and any other country interested in development and investing in our community.
Participates in international trade related conferences and trade shows for the purpose of attracting business and industry to our region.
Serves as the international trade liaison with foreign organizations.
Assists potential investors by organizing meeting agendas and providing regional economic and/or demographic data/maps.
Serves as conduit between private sectors and City Council for recommendation of economic policy.
Directs the development and maintenance of data on City of Laredo home page on the internet.
Represents City of Laredo in local and international forums by participating in meetings, conferences and/or trade shows.
Attends City Council meetings and takes necessary action regarding Council agenda items.
Works with an Advisory Committee made up of industry representatives that will make recommendations to the department and the City Council on policy changes needed to reflect the industry needs.
Briefs and advises City management, Mayor, and City Council regarding economic development and redevelopment projects, small business, tax increment financing zones, economic development corporations, and industrial districts in person and through written reports.
Participates in the development and administration of the budget for the tax increment zones, economic development corporations, and industrial districts.
Collaborates the City Manager, the Mayor, and city Council to define and articulate economic development visions, goals, strategies, and policies to formulate and implement strategic plans appropriate to the needs of the city and its citizens.
Educates economic development clients on City processes and helps facilitate the development process while working with City staff to balance the needs of economic development, permitting processes and City standards.
Develops and manages programs to assist new and expanding businesses in identifying resources and to provide financial incentive to encourage companies to locate in priority areas.
Develops and negotiates agreements between the City, private developers, and other governmental entities for economic development projects.
Will be required to drive a City vehicle for City business use.
Performs other duties as assigned.

Knowledge Of

COMPETENCIES FOR SUCCESSFUL PERFORMANCE OF JOB DUTIES

Knowledge of public and business administration as it relates to functions, needs, policies, laws, ordinances, and regulations governing the City.
Knowledge of the methods and practices of assembling, analyzing, and presenting statistical data.
Knowledge of grant writing and application preparation.

Ability to maintain good planning and organizational skills.

Ability to maintain harmonious and effective working relationships with other employees and respond to general public complaints.
Ability to effectively communicate both orally and in writing in the English language and orally in the Spanish language.
Ability to project a positive and professional image of the City of Laredo.

Integral Senior Living is looking for an Executive Director to join our community Discovery Commons San Ramon.

About The Opportunity

Does working for the leader in the Senior Housing industry sound like the professional challenge you are seeking? Integral Senior Living offers significant opportunities for growth and career advancement. Employees are encouraged to take responsibility for their own careers. You'll be working with incredible individuals with boundless creativity.

Responsibilities

Responsible for the operation and management of the community in accordance with the standards and guidelines of the Owners/Board of Directors, Integral Management Group, and federal/state/local laws and regulations.

Scion Executive Search has been engaged to conduct an immediate search for an Executive Director
for our client, a national nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing research, education, and public awareness surrounding skin health and dermatologic disease. Through strategic partnerships with leading researchers, physicians, academic institutions, donors, and volunteers, they accelerate innovative research while improving the lives of individuals affected by skin disease.

We are seeking a collaborative, mission-driven professional to lead the organization through its next chapter of growth, ensuring operational excellence, financial sustainability, and continued impact across research, fundraising, and organizational leadership. This full-time, hybrid role (3 days/week in office) is based in New York, New York.

Position Overview

Reporting to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director serves as the organization's chief executive, providing strategic, operational, and financial leadership while championing the mission of the organization. This leader will oversee daily operations, cultivate donor and institutional relationships, partner closely with the Board, and lead fundraising initiatives that support the organization's long-term success.

The Executive Director will manage a lean, high-performing team, including a Project Manager, while serving as the organization's primary ambassador to donors, researchers, clinicians, corporate partners, and other external stakeholders.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

Executive Leadership & Strategy

Provide visionary leadership that advances the organization's mission, strategic priorities, and long-term sustainability.
Partner closely with the Board of Directors to establish organizational goals, priorities, and annual operating plans.
Translate strategic objectives into measurable initiatives and organizational outcomes.
Foster a collaborative, mission-focused culture centered on accountability, transparency, and continuous improvement.

Fundraising & External Relations

Lead the organization's fundraising strategy, including major gifts, corporate partnerships, foundations, and special events.
Cultivate, steward, and expand relationships with donors, philanthropic partners, and key supporters.
Serve as the public face of the organization, representing the organization with researchers, medical institutions, corporate sponsors, and community stakeholders.
Identify opportunities to increase organizational visibility and philanthropic engagement.

Operations & Financial Leadership

Oversee the organization's daily operations while ensuring efficient, effective organizational performance.
Develop and manage the annual operating budget in partnership with the Board and external financial partners.
Ensure strong financial stewardship, reporting, compliance, and sound business practices.
Establish operational processes that support organizational growth and scalability.

Team Leadership

Lead, mentor, and develop a small, dedicated staff while fostering a collaborative and high-performing work environment.
Directly supervise the Project Manager and support professional growth across the organization.
Recruit, retain, and develop talent that strengthens organizational capacity.

Board Partnership & Governance

Serve as the primary liaison to the Board of Directors.
Prepare Board materials, reports, financial updates, and strategic recommendations.
Support Board committees and governance initiatives while ensuring effective communication and engagement.

QUALIFICATIONS:

Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred.
Progressive executive leadership experience within a nonprofit organization, foundation, healthcare, medical research, or related mission-driven environment.
Demonstrated success leading fundraising initiatives and cultivating major donor relationships.
Strong financial and operational management experience, including budgeting, planning, and organizational oversight.
Experience partnering with an engaged Board of Directors and navigating nonprofit governance.
Outstanding relationship-building, communication, and executive presence.
Strategic thinker with the ability to balance long-term vision and day-to-day execution.
Passion for advancing medical research, healthcare, or scientific innovation is strongly preferred.

The successful candidate will be:
A mission-driven executive who inspires confidence among donors, Board members, staff, and external partners.
A relationship builder who excels at cultivating meaningful partnerships and securing philanthropic support.
A strategic and operational leader capable of strengthening organizational infrastructure while driving future growth.
An effective communicator with exceptional executive presence and emotional intelligence.
A collaborative leader who empowers staff while maintaining accountability and high performance.

COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS:

In addition to offering a competitive salary in the range of $180,000 to $200,000 (commensurate with experience), this opportunity provides a comprehensive benefit package, to be disclosed.

new_jobs.description:
About Fubo:

FuboTV Inc. is a consumer-first live TV streaming company that is defining the future of TV.

Ranked among Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies (2026) and the Financial Times’ The Americas’ Fastest-Growing Companies (2026, 2025), FuboTV owns Hulu + Live TV (entertainment), Fubo (sports) and Molotov (entertainment and sports), which stream in markets around the globe.

Our Mission:

Our mission is to deliver premium sports, news and entertainment programming through a best-in-class user experience that offers greater choice, flexibility and value.

About the Role:

This role is a New York City based HYBRID position. Candidates must be located in NYC, and willing to come into the office on a hybrid basis, three times a week (Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday).*

Fubo is currently seeking a Chief of Staff to the CEO to serve as a strategic partner in a new position. This is a high impact role that will help transform vision into execution across the company. It’s best suited for someone that thrives at the intersection of strategy and execution, excels at solving complex business problems, creates alignment across teams, and helps ensure the company’s most important priorities become a reality.

This is not a traditional leadership role with a defined team. It is a role built on influence without authority, working across the entire organization, acting as an extension of the CEO. You will bring structure to ambiguity, connecting teams, identifying opportunities for growth and improvement, and ensuring the leadership team is operating at its highest level.

What you will do:

Act as a trusted partner to the CEO to define, refine, and execute the company’s strategic agenda
Develop agenda’s for CEO lead meetings (Senior leadership meetings, board meetings, off-sites, etc.); Work with the CEO to identify key themes and topics, giving input as needed from key stakeholders
Coordinate other CEO lead events, and assist in developing content and agendas
Help translate long-term vision into clear initiatives, measurable objectives, and execution plans
Identify emerging opportunities and risks through market research, competitor analysis, and financial analysis
Ensure all materials shared with the CEO are succinct, accurate, current, and verified
Work with the finance team to support long-range planning, annual planning, and resource allocation
Build business cases and financial models to evaluate strategic investments and growth opportunities
Monitor the company’s performance against strategic objectives and identify areas requiring intervention
Help develop action plans and mobilizing resources to drive resolution for critical business challenges
Help improve how the executive team operates through better planning, decision making, and follow through
Partner closely with various business leaders across the organization to accelerate initiatives, remove roadblocks, and ensure teams are aligned by removing friction between teams, clarifying priorities, resolving dependencies, and improving communication
Build trust across organizations without relying on formal authority
Lead confidential and high priority CEO initiatives including partnerships, organizational design, investor preparation, board materials, and business transformation efforts
Help create clarity in ambiguous situations
Partner with People Ops and Talent Acquisition teams to support leadership development, succession planning, and organizational design
Represent the CEO in select areas and initiatives as needed
Anticipate business needs, challenges, and opportunities, and proactively provide insights and recommendations

Who you are:

You combine strategic thinking with practical execution
You’re comfortable discussing market expansion, financial performance, organizational design, and execution details
10+ years of experience in strategy consulting, corporate strategy, project management, business operations, or related leadership roles; preferably within the digital media, technology, or subscription space
Demonstrated success leading complex, cross-functional initiatives from ideation to execution
Experience working with executive leadership in fast moving organizations
A strong financial background with the ability to interpret data and build robust business cases
Outstanding analytical and problem solving skills
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, including written narratives, presentations, briefing materials, and leadership updates
High emotional intelligence, sound judgement, and absolute discretion
Highly structured and organised, with the ability to create clarity in varying situations
The ability to move comfortably between executive strategy and hands-on execution
Extensive experience working cross functionally with all levels of global business units (finance, marketing, technology, product, design, business development, etc.)
A proven ability to navigate complex stakeholder environments

Perks & Benefits:

At Fubo, you will have the opportunity to personally influence what live media looks like after it goes through its next big transformation
Fubo provides a highly competitive compensation based on experience and market standards
Robust benefit package including Health/Dental/Vision coverage 401k, Life Insurance, and commuter benefits
Free Premium Fubo Account
Unlimited PTO days and regular company-wide activities
Fubo's main headquarters are located in Midtown Manhattan, with offices in Paris, and Bangalore
Fubo is an e-verified company

Fubo’s minimum base salary for this role if hired in New York City is $185,000 per year; maximum base salary for this role is $250,000 per year. Additionally, this role is eligible to participate in Fubo's equity plan, annual cash bonus plans, unlimited PTO, and a full range of medical benefits. Working closely with IT and Privacy teams, the Executive Director will define the standards, frameworks, and capabilities required to unlock greater value from ELC's first-party data assets and enable future growth across paid and owned channels. The role will establish a common approach to consumer data management, audience governance and segmentation, creating the foundations required for more effective marketing execution.

Establish ELC's Consumer Data Strategy & Foundations
Define the enterprise vision and roadmap for consumer data capabilities aligned to ELC's broader marketing transformation.
Establish global consumer data standards, taxonomies, and foundational frameworks that can be adopted consistently across brands and affiliates.
Partner closely with Privacy, Legal, Security, and IT teams to ensure consumer data practices align with global regulatory requirements and internal governance standards.
Partner closely with other functions, regions and affiliates to define and prioritize global use cases to inform IT roadmaps.
Drive value realization from consumer data investments through strong adoption, governance, and cross-functional collaboration.

Define Global Audience Segmentation Frameworks

Lead development of ELC's global segmentation framework, creating a common approach that can be adopted across brands, categories, and markets.
Define methodologies for identifying high-value consumer groups and monitor performance insights over time.
Partner to ensure segmentation and audience capabilities are embedded within media planning, audience strategy, and measurement frameworks.

Build Global Consumer Data Operating Model

Define the future-state operating model for consumer data across global, regional, and local teams.
Establish global governance frameworks for audience creation, management, activation, and measurement across global EBS and media agency partners.
Develop scalable ways of working that improve accessibility, usability, and adoption of consumer data capabilities across the enterprise.

Capability Building & Organizational Enablement

Build enterprise understanding of consumer data, audience management, segmentation, identity, and governance principles.
Create capability-building programs, playbooks, and communities of practice that accelerate adoption of audience-led marketing across ELC.
Establish a global community of consumer data practitioners to share best practices, learnings, and innovation opportunities.

The company’s products are sold in approximately 150 countries and territories under brand names including: Estée Lauder, Aramis, Clinique, Lab Series, Origins, M·A·C, La Mer, Bobbi Brown Cosmetics, Aveda, Jo Malone London, Bumble and bumble, Darphin Paris, TOM FORD, Smashbox, AERIN Beauty, Le Labo, Editions de Parfums Frédéric Malle, GLAMGLOW, KILIAN PARIS, Too Faced, Dr.Jart+, the DECIEM family of brands, including The Ordinary and NIOD, and BALMAIN Beauty.

Description

Role Overview

The Executive Director, Influencer Marketing partners with global brands and affiliates to operationalize and elevate influencer marketing across all ELC brands. This role is responsible for developing frameworks to identify and engage different tiers of influencers, enabling the selection and adoption of the right global technology platforms for discovery and performance measurement, and designing the operating model including agency partnerships and market collaboration that supports consistent, scalable execution.

The Executive Director ensures influencer marketing is embedded as a strategic enabler of our new global Growth Marketing practice, delivering brand equity and measurable business impact across the enterprise.

Qualifications

Key Responsibilities

Influencer Strategy & Frameworks

Define enterprise influencer marketing strategy, with a clear global vision to accelerate adoption and value realization across brands and affiliates.
Establish global influencer tiering models, playbooks and activation frameworks, to outline best practices for brands and affiliates to embed influencer into their overall brand ecosystem with clear roles and KPIs.
Define global investment guidance frameworks aligned to market maturity, channel roles, and category growth ambitions.
Build competitive intelligence and benchmarking frameworks including share of voice, creator mix, and performance benchmarks.
Collaborate closely with media and social commerce teams to embed influencer best practices and frameworks as part of an integrated communications strategy.

Technology, Measurement & Performance

Partner closely with IT and global affiliates to identify and scale global influencer technology platforms for discovery, management, activation and measurement.
Establish global affiliate technologies globally (eg. Shop my) and work closely with performance marketing team to embed insights into our total performance approach.
Define global KPI frameworks and reporting standards across brand and performance objectives, with clear monitoring to track global performance and adoption.
Define standardized global KPI taxonomy across brand, engagement, commerce and ROI metrics.

Operating Model & Governance

Enable the new OneELC global operating model for influencer marketing, defining roles and responsibilities across global brands, regions, and local teams.
Establish global roster of influencer agencies and work with global affiliates to embed as part of their local ways of working.
Partner with legal to establish global agency contracting framework and incentive model to unlock full potential from agency partners.
Establish clear governance, guidelines, and best practices covering brand safety, compliance, disclosures, contracts, and usage rights in close partnership with global legal teams.

Market Enablement & Capability Building

Develop global influencer community across ELC, cascading global best practices, learnings and feedback on global frameworks.
Build out global capability building materials to level up the organization and build excellence in influencer strategy and execution.
Build and mentor a high‑performing global team and extended community of influencer practitioners.

Pay Range

Anticipated Base Salary Range $158,650.00 to $272,700.00 (Depending on qualifications, skills, experience and/or budget), based on a 40 hour work week (range to be scaled accordingly). In addition, The Estée Lauder Companies offers a variety of benefits to eligible employees, including health insurance coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance), wellness and family support programs, life and disability insurance, retirement savings plans, education-related programs, paid holidays and vacation time. In addition, the Company maintains highly competitive incentive compensation programs (role eligibility may vary based on terms of the respective plan(s)).

You may be eligible to participate in the applicable Commission/Bonus Plan, under the plan guidelines in effect at the time of hire. Additional details regarding the commission plan will be provided as part of your onboarding.

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
Director of Operations

Brooklyn, New York | Hybrid

Compensation:
$175,000–$200,000, commensurate with experience, plus a performance-based bonus opportunity of up to 20% of base salary tied to agreed-upon individual and Firm performance objectives.

Help Build New York's Premier Boutique Business Litigation Firm

At Alisme Law, we believe exceptional legal service begins with exceptional operations.
We represent businesses, business owners, partners, and shareholders in complex business disputes. Our clients trust us because we make sense of complexity through disciplined systems, thoughtful strategy, and consistent execution.
As our Firm continues to grow, we are intentionally building an organization that is every bit as exceptional operationally as it is legally.
We are seeking an experienced Director of Operations to serve as the operational leader of our Firm. This is an opportunity for someone who enjoys building teams, developing leaders, improving systems, and creating an organization where people consistently perform at a high level.
If you are energized by building organizations, not simply managing them, we would love to meet you.

Where We Are Going

Alisme Law has built a strong reputation for helping clients navigate complex business disputes. Our next chapter is focused on building a scalable organization capable of sustained growth while maintaining the personalized service, professionalism, and trust our clients expect.
Over the next several years, we will continue to strengthen our leadership team, refine our operating systems, and invest in our people so the Firm can grow intentionally without sacrificing its culture or client experience.
We are entering an exciting stage of growth. Our Firm has built a strong foundation and is now investing in the leadership, systems, and infrastructure necessary to support our next phase. The Director of Operations will inherit responsibility for leading our administrative team while helping build the management structure, operational processes, and organizational capacity needed for sustained growth.
We are looking for an operational leader who is excited to help build that future.

About the Role

Reporting directly to the Owner, you will lead the Firm's day-to-day business operations by developing people, strengthening accountability, improving systems, and ensuring operational excellence across the organization.
This is not an office manager position. It is a senior leadership role for someone who enjoys coaching people, improving organizations, solving operational challenges, and building high-performing teams.
While the Owner establishes the Firm's strategic direction, the Director of Operations is responsible for leading the execution of that vision through people, systems, and operational discipline.

Your First-Year Priorities

During your first year, your primary focus will be to build the operational foundation for the Firm's next stage of growth.

Build a High-Performing Team

Recruit, onboard, develop, and coach exceptional people. Create a culture where every team member understands expectations, receives regular feedback, and is held accountable to measurable standards.

Build a Scalable Operating Model

Strengthen the Firm's operating systems by documenting and improving core processes, implementing meaningful KPIs, establishing consistent meeting rhythms, and ensuring operational excellence across the organization.

Deliver an Exceptional Client Experience

Improve the operational systems that support the client journey, from intake through matter completion, by creating consistency, responsiveness, and professionalism at every stage.

What You Will Own

To accomplish these priorities, you will lead the Firm's business operations, including:
People Operations
Business Operations
Client Experience
Operational Finance
Technology and Vendor Management

Your success will be measured not by how much work you personally complete, but by how effectively the organization performs under your leadership.

Decision-Making Authority

We believe leadership requires both responsibility and authority.
Within established budgets, policies, and strategic objectives, you will have the authority to:

Lead, coach, evaluate, and hold administrative team members accountable while ensuring firm-wide compliance with the Firm's operational systems, policies, and performance standards.
Recommend hiring, promotions, compensation adjustments, and terminations.
Manage recruiting, onboarding, and performance management.
Improve and implement operational systems, workflows, and standard operating procedures.
Select and manage vendors and recommend technology solutions.
Establish and monitor operational KPIs and management reporting.
Resolve day-to-day operational issues independently.
Lead strategic operational initiatives from planning through implementation.

The Owner retains responsibility for legal strategy, client representation, annual budgeting, pricing, compensation strategy, and other major strategic decisions.

As you develop a deep understanding of the Firm, earn the Owner's trust, and build a strong operational foundation, the Owner intends to progressively delegate responsibility for the Firm's day-to-day operations. The long-term vision is for the Director of Operations to lead the Firm's business operations, allowing the Owner to focus primarily on legal strategy, client relationships, business development, and the Firm's long-term growth.

Operational Financial Leadership

You will oversee the Firm's operational financial processes by ensuring disciplined billing, timekeeping, collections, trust replenishment, operational reporting, and KPI management.
You will partner closely with the Owner and the Firm's outside accounting professionals on budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, and business planning. While you will play an important role in supporting the Firm's financial health, responsibility for financial strategy remains with the Owner.

Client Experience

The Director of Operations owns the systems and processes that support an exceptional client experience. This includes intake, onboarding, communication standards, service workflows, billing experience, and client feedback systems.
You will work in partnership with the Owner to continuously improve the client experience while recognizing that legal strategy and attorney-client representation remain the responsibility of the Firm's attorneys.

The Director is responsible for establishing, monitoring, and improving the Firm's operational standards across all personnel. Attorneys remain responsible for legal judgment and client representation, while the Director oversees compliance with the Firm's operational systems and procedures.

What Success Looks Like After 12 Months

A successful first year will include meaningful progress in the following areas:

Leadership & People

A structured recruiting, onboarding, and performance management process is fully implemented.
Every employee has clear expectations, measurable KPIs, and regular coaching.
Leadership capacity is strengthened by developing team members, establishing clear accountability, and ensuring operational issues are addressed at the appropriate level within the organization.

Operations

Core operational workflows are documented, standardized, and consistently followed.
A meaningful operational KPI dashboard is implemented and reviewed regularly.
Weekly leadership meetings and quarterly planning sessions operate with consistency, accountability, and follow-through.
Operational decisions are supported by accurate reporting and meaningful performance data.

Client Experience

Client communication standards are consistently met.
Intake, onboarding, billing, and matter completion processes become more predictable and efficient.
Client feedback is actively measured and used to improve service delivery.

Business Operations

Billing, collections, and operational reporting processes operate consistently and efficiently.
Operational technology supports productivity and continuous improvement.
The Firm's leadership team has greater visibility into performance and operational trends through consistent reporting and accountability.

Compensation & Benefits

We believe exceptional performance should be rewarded. Our compensation package includes:

Competitive base salary of $175,000–$200,000, commensurate with experience.
Performance-based bonus opportunity of up to 20% of base salary, tied to agreed-upon individual and Firm performance objectives.
Hybrid work environment. During onboarding, the Director is expected to work primarily from our Brooklyn office to build relationships, learn the Firm, and understand our operations. Thereafter, we anticipate a flexible hybrid schedule with regular in-office leadership presence, generally one to two days per week, depending on business needs and Firm priorities. Additional in-person time may be expected during key hiring, planning, training, and leadership initiatives.
Paid Time Off and paid holidays.
Professional development opportunities.
The opportunity to help shape the future of a growing law firm.

Our Hiring Process

We hire carefully because our people are our greatest competitive advantage.
To apply please email the following to info@alismelaw.com
:
Your résumé.
A brief email explaining why this opportunity interests you.
A video (no longer than three minutes) answering the following questions:
Why does this opportunity excite you?
Tell us about an operational transformation you personally led that had a measurable impact.

Please include the phrase "Systems Create Freedom" in the subject line of your application.
Because attention to detail, communication, and follow-through are essential to success in this role, applicants who submit complete application materials and follow the requested instructions will receive priority consideration.

Please do not contact the Firm by telephone or visit our office regarding this position.

Westminster Communities of Florida, a nationally recognized, faith-based not-for-profit organization, is seeking an Executive Director
in St. Augustine, Florida.

Founded in 1954, Westminster Communities of Florida is the largest not-for-profit provider of senior living in the state dedicated to a Mission and ministry of service to older adults. We proudly serve more than 7,000 residents throughout Florida, offering an active lifestyle paired with a full continuum of exceptional care — including independent living, assisted living, memory support, skilled nursing and rehabilitation, home care, pharmacy services, and affordable housing for low-income seniors. Join a team where you can make life great for residents and team members alike!

What You'll Do

Leadership & Operations

Implement and maintain policies established by the Board of Directors and Community Support Services offices
Uphold organizational standards and ensure fulfillment of all contractual obligations to residents, including entrance fee and continuing care agreements
Ensure compliance with all applicable laws and regulations, including AHCA licensure requirements, state Life Plan Community/CCRC statutes, and CMS quality standards where applicable
Maintain survey readiness and support continuous quality improvement across all levels of care

Team & Department Leadership

Select, develop, evaluate, and lead a cross-functional leadership team — including Nursing/Health Services, Dining and Nutrition Services, Life Enrichment/Activities, Plant Operations and Maintenance, Marketing and Sales, and Social Services/Resident Care Coordination in partnership with Community Support Services Directors/Coordinators.
Provide overall direction, coordination, and evaluation of community staff, including hiring, training, performance management, and resolving team member concerns
Foster a culture that supports retention and engagement in a competitive senior living labor market

Financial & Business Operations

Recommend and manage the annual capital and operational budget, following Community Support Services accounting procedures
Monitor budget performance, occupancy trends, and revenue cycle health; submit monthly variance reports and approve major purchases and invoices
Apply Life Plan/CCRC business acumen — understanding entrance fee models, reserve requirements, and actuarial considerations unique to Life Plan Community operations

Resident & Community Engagement

Champion person-centered, aging-in-place philosophies that honor resident choice and independence across the continuum
Maintain community grounds and physical properties in excellent condition to support residents' quality of life
Build strong relationships with local community groups, agencies, and organizations to support community engagement and philanthropic partnerships

What You Bring

Education:
Bachelor's degree in a health-related or business field required; Master's degree preferred

Experience

Minimum of 5 years of successful management experience in the retirement/senior living industry, preferably within a multi-facility or CCRC/Life Plan Community organization
Current Florida Nursing Home Administrator's License, or ability to obtain one promptly
Familiarity with AHCA survey processes and CCRC regulatory requirements preferred

Skills & Attributes

Strong ability to motivate and communicate effectively with residents, families, and staff across multiple levels of care
Comfortable reading and interpreting financial statements, occupancy reports, and CCRC-specific financial models
Self-motivated with a proven ability to set and achieve goals
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present confidently to leadership, boards, and community groups
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; able to interpret complex data and technical information
Resilient and adaptable under pressure, with the ability to navigate the mental and emotional demands of a dynamic senior living leadership role
Genuine passion for the aging services mission and improving the lived experience of older adults

Why Join Us

This is an opportunity to lead with purpose — shaping a community where residents feel valued, supported, and at home, and where staff are equipped to do their best work. You'll be backed by a strong Community Support Services infrastructure and a shared commitment to excellence across the full continuum of senior living.
